3. When --provider is NOT pinned, existing escalation behavior is unchanged (regression test).

4. --max-tier cap continues to work as an explicit override and is honored even when the provider-affinity filter would allow a higher tier.

5. With ddx-e59e7fe8 and this bead landed, 'ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx' (no --model, no --max-tier) either completes cleanly at cheap tier or emits a clear 'no tier resolves' diagnostic — no opaque 404 escalation.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"5328120be55ab6af07a5cd35c7dc7f7a22f30f96","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:58:05.964534969Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3c5ba7cc","depends_on_id":"ddx-e59e7fe8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:58:11Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-3c5ba7cc","depends_on_id":"ddx-8610020e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:07:26Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-3c5ba7cc","depends_on_id":"ddx-0486e601","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:14:30Z"}],"description":"## Problem

When 'ddx work --provider P' is invoked with a pinned provider and the cheap-tier attempt fails (for any reason: execution_failed, land_conflict, no_changes repeated, etc.), DDx escalates to the standard or smart tier using the project config's tier model overrides WITHOUT checking whether those escalation-target models actually exist on the pinned provider.

Observed on eitri 2026-04-17 (DDx v0.6.0-alpha22):

    .ddx/config.yaml:
      agent:
        routing:
          profile_priority: [cheap]
          model_overrides:
            cheap:  qwen/qwen3.6
            fast:   kimi/k2.5
            smart:  minimax/minimax-m2.7

    $ ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx
    # cheap-tier attempt on vidar-omlx fails (for whatever reason — in this
    # session it was catalog-missing model on vidar-omlx; see ddx-e59e7fe8)
    # smart-tier escalation tries minimax/minimax-m2.7 — also routes to
    # vidar-omlx because of --provider pinning — fails with 404 'Model not
    # found' because vidar-omlx has MiniMax-M2.5-MLX-4bit, not m2.7.
    #
    # Final outcome: 'escalation exhausted: ...404 Not Found...'

Evidence: .ddx/executions/20260418T005017-ac81ac97/.

## What escalation should do instead

When --provider P is pinned, tier escalation options (in preference order):

1. **Restrict escalation to models that resolve on the pinned provider.**
   For each tier in profile_priority after the failing tier, check whether the
   tier's model_override resolves (via catalog) to a concrete ID that the
   pinned provider serves. If yes, use it. If no, skip that tier.

2. **If no higher tier resolves on the pinned provider, cap escalation.**
   Surface a clear diagnostic: 'escalation capped at cheap tier — smart
   (minimax/minimax-m2.7) does not resolve on provider vidar-omlx'. Honor
   --max-tier as a hard cap regardless of provider pinning.

3. **Do NOT silently widen to a different provider unless the user opts in.**
   'ddx work --provider P' is a pinning directive; spontaneously routing to
   openrouter because smart-tier doesn't fit on P violates the user's intent
   and leaks cost/quota to the wrong place.

4. **Separate case: --provider is NOT pinned (no flag).** Current behavior
   of picking a different provider for higher tiers is fine; this bead is
   only about the pinned case.

## Fix sketch

In cli/internal/agent/routing.go (or wherever tier escalation lives):

1. When the runner sees req.ProviderAffinity != '' (or equivalent pinned-
   provider signal), build the tier ladder by filtering to tiers whose
   model_override resolves on that provider via the catalog.

2. If the filtered ladder is empty (not even cheap resolves), fail fast with
   a route-plan error before claiming the bead, not after a 14s dispatch.

3. Emit a routing event on the bead recording the effective ladder, e.g.:
   {resolved_provider: 'vidar-omlx', effective_tiers: ['cheap'], capped_reason:
   'smart-tier minimax/minimax-m2.7 not served by vidar-omlx'}.

## Files likely touched

- cli/internal/agent/routing.go — tier escalation logic, provider-affinity filter
- cli/internal/agent/routing_test.go — regression: pinned provider + smart-tier override that doesn't resolve on that provider should cap at cheap, not 404 at dispatch
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go — the escalation loop; needs to read the effective ladder

## Depends on

- ddx-e59e7fe8 (catalog: omlx surface) — escalation-filter logic needs catalog-lookup to know what resolves where. Land that first or in the same change.

- Replace markdown section headings in the synthesized execute-bead prompt with XML-style tags for machine-significant structure
- Preserve the current human-readable content while making the prompt easier to parse, diff, and validate deterministically
- Define which sections are required, optional, and repeatable in the prompt template
- Keep the prompt aligned with the tracked execution manifest and future commit-provenance rendering
- Update the governing specs and contracts so the prompt-template contract is documented rather than only implied by code

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006 and any applicable execute-bead contract docs to define the prompt-template structure
- Align the execution-evidence lane docs so prompt.md is explicitly structured, machine-readable evidence

- Update prompt synthesis in cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go to emit XML-tagged sections

Manual verification: bash scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh /tmp/ddx-fixture-test --profile standard && cd /tmp/ddx-fixture-test && ddx bead ready # shows the seeded beads.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:50:22Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"36dbc04b113889d5089c94c24b87a1d55c67e188","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:44:52.913285252Z","description":"Multiple test/demo workflows need a clean ddx-initialized git repo without polluting this main project. Today each test creates its own t.TempDir() fixture; integration tests do ad-hoc setup; acceptance demos have nowhere to go.

USE CASES (current + planned)
- ADR-022 acceptance demo (Stage 4 of design-phase plan): run 2 workers, restart server mid-execution, verify worker resilience without polluting the ddx repo
- Integration tests that need a real .ddx/ directory + bead store + workers/ + executions/
- bd/br interchange tests
- Cross-project leak tests (need >1 project — already partially used in ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1 tests)
- Manual operator walkthroughs / demos for new features
- "What does ddx look like in a fresh project?" docs

DESIGN OPTIONS

Option 1: A SCRIPT that builds a fixture repo on demand
- scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh <dest> [--profile minimal|standard|multi-project|federated]
- Creates a tmp git repo, runs ddx init, optionally adds N seed beads, optionally registers projects
- Tests call it via testing.T helper; demos call it from the operator's shell
- Cleanup is operator/test responsibility (rm -rf <dest>)

Option 2: A FIXTURE-REPO BINARY (ddx fixture create)
- ddx fixture create <dest> --profile standard
- Same outputs as Option 1 but discoverable via --help
- Could be a hidden subcommand under ddx (Hidden: true) since it's dev infrastructure
- Uniform with rest of CLI

Option 3: GO TESTING HELPER (testing-only, not exposed to operator)
- testutil.NewFixtureRepo(t, profile) → projectPath
- Auto-cleanup via t.Cleanup()
- Doesn't help acceptance demos but cleanest for unit/integration tests

RECOMMEND: Hybrid. Option 1 (script) for demo + manual use; Option 3 (Go helper) wraps the script for tests so cleanup is automatic. Option 2 deferred unless operators ask for it.

PROFILES TO SUPPORT (initial)
- minimal: empty .ddx/, no beads
- standard: .ddx/ + 5 mixed-priority sample beads + sample personas + sample skills
- multi-project: 2 registered projects, each with their own beads (for cross-project tests)
- federated: hub + spoke setup (for ADR-007 federation tests)

NOT IN SCOPE
- Mocking the agent service (separate concern; tests use stub fizeau already)
- Pre-populating with real LLM execution traces (deterministic test runs use fixtures, not live LLMs)
- Auto-cleanup beyond t.Cleanup() (operators run rm -rf)

DEPS
- Independent; can land anytime.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T03:04:25.173822473Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T025022-29ef49b0","harness":"claude","input_tokens":60,"output_tokens":23076,"total_tokens":23136,"cost_usd":2.67096525,"duration_ms":841122,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T03:04:25.210188658Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=23136 cost_usd=2.6710"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-03T03:04:31.371009062Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:394:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:39:31.542862754Z","description":"Finding from fresh-eyes review of the run/try/work boundary plan.

Problem:
The plan says there should be one simple run command layered under try and work, but current governing docs and shipped skill references still describe ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, execute-loop, quorum harness flags, and agent-provider command surfaces as active user/automation primitives. Examples found during review include FEAT-006 Agent Service overview/responsibilities, FEAT-010 Layer 1 wording, and cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md persona/quorum/help examples. Parallel copied skill docs under .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx contain the same stale vocabulary.

Why this matters:
The docs are executable guidance for agents. Leaving legacy command names and flags in skills will keep agents routing work through the old surfaces even if the implementation adds top-level run/try/work.

Required fix:
Make the public command taxonomy bright-line and uniform across feature docs, skill docs, and CLI help: ddx run is the single agent execution primitive, ddx try layers retry/review policy over run, and ddx work layers bead orchestration over try. Agent-internal service commands may exist only as hidden/internal plumbing and must not be documented as workflow commands.

Acceptance:
- FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 no longer describe ddx agent run/execute-bead/execute-loop as public orchestration commands.
- DDx skill references in cli/internal/skills/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and .claude/skills/ddx use ddx run/try/work consistently.
- Quorum/compare/persona examples are either migrated to the simplified surface or removed if they imply DDx harness routing.
- A docs/skill grep test or validation step fails on stale public references to ddx agent run, execute-bead, execute-loop, --harnesses, or provider-routing examples outside explicit migration notes.","id":"ddx-52ce6249","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:docs","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-deb9be7e","priority":1,"spec_id":"review:power-passthrough-boundary-stale-commands","status":"closed","title":"review finding: remove stale ddx agent run and execute-bead command guidance","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:50:03.186901956Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:396:{"acceptance":"The DDx docs define `ddx agent execute-bead` as a clean-worktree workspace transform from `W` to `W'`; the bead is specified as the executable transform contract rather than a vague tracker ticket; DDx docs no longer assign governing-artifact resolution or prompt construction to DDx at runtime; a generic bead execution-readiness validation surface is specified with clear DDx-owned vs HELIX-owned checks; the docs define the success/failure state machine including structural validation, required post-run checks, preserve-on-failure evidence, and rebase+fast-forward landing on success; and the contract explicitly remains valid for future multi-project `ddx server` worker pools rather than assuming a single served repo","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-09T22:00:19.106711764Z","description":"<context-digest>
Review area: execute-bead semantics, executable bead contracts, readiness validation, and DDx/HELIX boundary ownership. Evidence covers FEAT-006, FEAT-010, FEAT-013, SD-012, server-driven execution discussions, and cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go.
</context-digest>
Define DDx bead execution around the functional model `W' = execute(bead, W)`, where `W` is a clean git-backed workspace input state and `ddx agent execute-bead` materializes that state in an isolated worktree before mutation.

## Required design outcomes
1. Clarify that `ddx agent execute-bead` starts from a resolved base revision, treats the caller's dirty worktree as outside the transform, checkpoints it separately, and executes only against the isolated worktree state.
2. Define the bead as the executable transform contract: the bead must carry the instruction/prompt, acceptance, declared context inputs, execution policy, landing/preservation policy, and post-run validation policy needed for replayable execution.
3. Unwind DDx designs that currently imply DDx derives governing artifacts, prompt construction, or workflow policy from the live document graph at execution time. DDx should provide composable graph/exec tools, not hidden workflow orchestration.
4. Define a generic DDx execution-readiness validation surface for beads, such as `ddx bead validate --execution` or equivalent inspection semantics, that checks structural executability without embedding HELIX-specific policy.
5. Specify the success/failure state machine around execution: structural validation, agent execution, post-run validity checks / required executions, preserve-on-failure evidence semantics, and land-on-success semantics.
6. Make the automation boundary explicit: future humans, HELIX flows, or `ddx server` worker pools must consume the explicit bead contract and validator output rather than relying on hidden prompt assembly or runtime artifact inference.
7. Preserve room for multi-project server execution: the contract must not assume a single served repo or a single server process, and must remain valid when project-scoped worktrees are managed by a multi-project `ddx server`.
8. Make HELIX's responsibility explicit: HELIX should author the executable bead contract and create a new contract document in `docs/helix/02-design/contracts/` that defines the DDx/HELIX boundary for executable bead execution. The contracts directory does not currently exist in this repo, so this work must establish it.

## Spec and design scope
- Reconcile FEAT-006 agent-service workflow with the workspace-transform model
- Reconcile FEAT-010 execution-definition language so execution selection and required checks are explicit input or separately composed query output, not hidden bead-context derivation
- Preserve FEAT-013's substrate-not-orchestration boundary while still supporting async server-managed worker pools
- Align SD-012 git-awareness wording with the actual clean-input/worktree model, checkpoint semantics, and preserve-vs-land outcomes
- Establish how the generic execution-readiness surface is consumed by future automation without embedding HELIX policy in DDx

## Expected artifact work
- New contract under `docs/helix/02-design/contracts/` for the executable bead boundary
- Updates to the affected feature / solution design docs so they all describe the same execution contract
- Follow-on design beads for server-managed supervision and multi-project server topology
- Follow-on implementation beads only after the contract is settled","execution-eligible":true,"id":"ddx-5448b39c","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:planning","kind:architecture","area:agent","area:bead","area:docs"],"priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Define execute-bead workspace-transform contract and readiness validation","updated_at":"2026-04-10T16:14:55.073044325Z"}

Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate, 1 the poll-interval-zero exit, 1 the routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated 14 times. The user undercounted the smell by ~5×. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick → Gate → Claim → Execute → Classify → Reviewing → Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   — Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   — Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  — Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes ~30 lines: pick → guard → claim → attemptWithHeartbeat → applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming

Package = try (not try_bead — Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface (ddx run / try / work) already correct; C13 also ADDS ddx try as a new CLI command (FEAT-010 named it but it doesn't exist yet).

  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation (absorbs ddx-cfedee8e ladder + ddx-a8718bec reviewer cost + ddx-dc157075 tactical work-stay-alive). Includes downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes 30s default for ddx work --poll-interval.
  C10 ddx work stay-alive default + transient idle substate (subsumes ddx-dc157075 fully)
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per §3 of proposal + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first

.ddx/beads.jsonl:492:{"acceptance":"1. (DONE) Wire-level evidence exists: /tmp/vidar-omlx-wire.jsonl captured via AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 on v0.3.12.

2. (DONE) Root cause classified: ddx-agent-side bug — the library includes Anthropic-specific 'thinking' field in requests to non-Anthropic openai-compat providers, which causes omlx to silently terminate the SSE stream after the first frame.

3. Upstream bead filed in ~/Projects/agent with the wire evidence, concrete fix direction, and acceptance criteria.

4. When upstream lands the fix, a chore bead bumps ddx-agent and this bead's retry scenario (ddx work --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6) produces either a closing status or a genuine provider/model error — NOT 'unexpected end of JSON input'.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:55:55.679619615Z","description":"## Status: classified — ddx-agent bug (not DDx, not omlx server)

Wire-level evidence captured 2026-04-18 with v0.3.12's AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 (upstream bead agent-941e7e42, landed in this session). Evidence: /tmp/vidar-omlx-wire.jsonl (3 events for a single failing attempt).

## Root cause

ddx-agent is sending an Anthropic-specific 'thinking' field in the OpenAI-compatible request body:

    POST http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions
    Content-Type: application/json
    body (18989 bytes, includes):
      "stream":true,
      "stream_options":{"include_usage":true},
      "thinking":{"budget_tokens":8192,"type":"enabled"},
      "tools":[... 6 tools ...],
      "model":"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit"

vidar-omlx opens a valid SSE stream (HTTP 200, Content-Type: text/event-stream), emits the first assistant-role delta frame correctly, then silently terminates the stream after 186 bytes without writing data: [DONE] or an error frame. The OpenAI Go client parses the one valid frame, hits stream termination, and surfaces 'unexpected end of JSON input'.

The 'thinking' field is Anthropic extended-thinking config. It is NOT in the OpenAI chat-completions schema. omlx (OpenAI-compatible) has no handler for it; the server reaches a silent error path in its generation pipeline when it encounters it in the processed request.

## Responsibility

ddx-agent owns per-flavor request serialization. The library must use Provider.DetectedFlavor() (landed in v0.3.12 via agent-92f0f324) to gate which fields are included in the wire body. Non-Anthropic providers must NOT see 'thinking'.

This bead now tracks the DDx-side work to:
1. Confirm the classification and hand off to upstream via a concrete agent-repo bead.
2. Retry vidar-omlx once the upstream fix lands in a v0.3.13 bump.

Upstream bead to file in ~/Projects/agent: 'provider: strip non-portable fields (thinking) from non-Anthropic request bodies using DetectedFlavor() gate'.

## Classification was correct to wait for evidence

Prior to this capture, the bead's framing (upstream fix assumed) could have been over-scoped. The fresh-eyes review (codex + opus, 2026-04-17) flagged that the 'direct curl works' isolation was insufficient — 'unexpected end of JSON input' is equally consistent with (a) DDx sending a malformed request, (b) ddx-agent parser defect, (c) omlx server defect. The wire capture shows:

- (a) partially true: DDx IS passing a preset that triggers the thinking field, but this is a library responsibility to gate by destination flavor.
- (b) false: the parser reads what it gets; it errors because there is literally no more data to parse, not because the data is malformed.
- (c) partially true: omlx silently terminates instead of returning a 400 with 'unknown field: thinking', which is poor error handling — but the root cause is the library sending a field omlx shouldn't receive.

The layer that MUST fix this is ddx-agent: the library builds the request; the library knows the destination flavor (agent-92f0f324 DetectedFlavor); the library must strip fields that would be ignored-or-fatal at the destination.

## Evidence

/tmp/vidar-omlx-wire.jsonl preserved. Three events: request (body captured, Authorization redacted), response headers (HTTP 200, text/event-stream), response body (186 bytes, one valid assistant-role delta frame, then EOF).

Failed bead from retry: agent-b1c2d3e4 (in ~/Projects/agent; not a DDx bead). Same failure shape as the prior two attempts on Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit and Qwen3-Coder-Next-MLX-4bit — confirms model-independent + project-independent.

## DDx-side action

1. File the upstream bead in ~/Projects/agent with the wire evidence inlined. See agent-<hash> filed in the same commit as this update.
2. Close THIS bead as 'evidence-gathered, scope-handed-off' per its own AC #3.
3. When upstream ships the fix in a v0.3.13+ bump, file a chore bead for the dep bump and retry vidar-omlx.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"agent","resolved_model":"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit","route_reason":"direct-override","fallback_chain":[],"base_url":"http://vidar:1235/v1"}","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:56:55.52607894Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent model=Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit reason=direct-override"},{"actor":"erik","body":"agent: provider error: unexpected end of JSON input

3. runAgentExecuteLoopImpl calls parseExecuteLoopSpec once and uses the returned spec for execution inputs.
4. ADR-022 inline-only behavior preserved: execute-loop always runs inline; --local is a deprecated no-op.

The execute-loop/work CLI path still extracts each flag into local variables and threads those values through bespoke inline logic. The root bead requires a single cobra-to-spec conversion so execution fields are not re-maintained at every boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 reads ProjectRoot/from/routing/review/cost/timeout/power flags into local variables, then cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1759-1777 manually projects a subset into config.CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadRuntime. Control-plane flags are mixed with execution fields in the same RunE body.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd, treatPassthroughAsOpaque) returning (executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, executeloop.DispatchOptions, error).
- Make runAgentExecuteLoopImpl call parseExecuteLoopSpec once, then use the spec for inline execution inputs and the dispatch options for --json/--local behavior.
- Keep current ADR-022 behavior: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1509-1511 says execute-loop always runs inline and --local is a deprecated no-op. If a live HTTP submit helper still exists, make it accept and marshal ExecuteLoopSpec directly; otherwise do not resurrect server-submit mode.
- Preserve ddx try behavior: tryTargetBeadID still forces Once true and FromRev still reaches ExecuteBeadRuntime.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change route validation semantics or auto-infer tier behavior.
- Do not change server REST/GraphQL handlers; separate children own those transports.
- Do not reshape LabelFilter.","id":"ddx-76cf71f4","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:server","kind:refactor","prevention","triage:needs-investigation"],"parent":"ddx-29058e2a","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"cmd: parse execute-loop flags into ExecuteLoopSpec","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:12:45.904500313Z"}

Setting persona_bindings: code-reviewer: reliability-guardian in .ddx/config.yaml emits a stderr warning at load time. All deprecation warnings are stderr, not stdout. cd cli && go test ./... passes with updated persona tests.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:53:22Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1527076","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:11:58.764851918Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-77753c81","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"Before the 5 dropped personas are physically deleted (reliability-guardian, simplicity-architect, data-driven-optimizer, product-discovery-analyst, product-manager-minimalist), emit deprecation warnings so users who have them bound in .ddx/config.yaml get a clear signal to migrate.

Changes:
1. In the persona loader (cli/internal/persona/ or cli/cmd/persona.go), maintain an explicit deprecation list — persona names that still exist on disk but are scheduled for removal.
2. ddx persona list: mark deprecated personas with a (deprecated) suffix in the listing.
3. ddx persona show <name>: if <name> is deprecated, prepend a warning banner explaining the persona is scheduled for removal and pointing to a surviving persona (or to library/personas/README.md) as a migration target.
4. ddx agent run --persona <name>: if <name> is deprecated, emit a stderr warning (non-fatal) with the migration pointer. Do not error — users migrate on their own timeline.
5. Resolver for persona_bindings in .ddx/config.yaml: same stderr warning when a deprecated persona is bound. Do not error.

The warnings must be stderr, not stdout, so they don't poison piped output. The warning is non-fatal — the deprecated persona still loads and runs normally during the deprecation window.

One release later, the 'Delete dropped personas' bead removes the files; by then users have seen the warnings.

In-scope:
- cli/internal/persona/ (whichever file does loading/listing)
- cli/cmd/persona.go (the CLI subcommands)
- A small data structure (map or slice) of deprecated persona names with suggested replacements

Out-of-scope:
- Actually deleting the deprecated files (separate bead)
- Test fixtures — update as needed but keep minimal

.ddx/beads.jsonl:561:{"acceptance":"The design stack defines native `.agent/config.yaml` as the authoritative embedded-agent config source, documents precedence with environment / `.ddx` orchestration config / CLI overrides, and queues implementation work to remove DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror while preserving OpenRouter/native runtime compatibility","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-13T16:24:47Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3842205","closing_commit_sha":"9aab7a0f9f9e90b2ce4e2cb7ee694d7c4eee1c7f","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:40:58.751679274Z","description":"<context-digest>
Review area: embedded-agent configuration ownership. Evidence covers ddx's partial `agent.agent_runner` mirror in `.ddx/config.yaml`, the native `ddx-agent` config model in `.agent/config.yaml`, and the need to support existing provider/backends such as OpenRouter without duplicating or losing configuration surface.
</context-digest>
Replace DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror with native `ddx-agent` configuration loading.

## Goals
- Make `.agent/config.yaml` the authoritative config source for the embedded `agent` harness
- Define precedence between native agent config, environment variables, DDx orchestration config, and CLI overrides
- Remove or narrow DDx-owned embedded-agent config fields that duplicate native runtime config
- Ensure DDx `agent run`, `execute-bead`, and `execute-loop` can consume existing OpenRouter/native agent setups without bespoke DDx translation
- Add regression coverage for embedded-agent config resolution and override semantics","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"success

reason: parent bead ddx-7b753655 was decomposed into three focused child slices because it exceeds the size-check thresholds (>6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems; mixes backend GraphQL + frontend Svelte migration + Playwright e2e + clock-driven integration test as separate top-level deliverables; >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages — the existing workers panel +layout.svelte alone is 438 LOC).

Children created (parent=ddx-7b753655):

- 5a (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7b753655-20260503T064620-a387a207-28f710ee):
  Backend derived-view GraphQL workers query + resolver reading from
  s.workerIngest registry (cli/internal/server/worker_ingest.go).
  Adds schema fields: id, project, harness, state (freshness via
  freshnessState()), lastEventAt, mirrorFailuresCount,
  hadDroppedBackfill, currentBead, currentAttempt. Includes
  TestGraphQL_Workers_FreshnessFields and
  TestGraphQL_Workers_MultipleWorkersPerProject. ~150 LOC.

- 5b (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7b753655-20260503T064620-a387a207-86d32a20):
  Frontend Svelte workers panel migration at
  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/.
  Surfaces freshness indicator, duplicate-worker display, and the
  "trusted-peer reported, not authoritative" UI labeling. Includes
  Playwright e2e covering the new fields against fixture state.
  Depends on 5a. ~400 LOC.

- 5c (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7b753655-20260503T064620-a387a207-7888e046):
  Wired-in integration test TestWorkersPanel_LiveTransitions —
  starts a real worker, asserts connected→stale (after 2× probe
  interval)→disconnected (after 10× probe interval) using a
  clock-injection seam in worker_ingest.freshnessState(). Not
  fixture-based. Depends on 5a. ~150 LOC + small clock seam.

Dependency edges added: 5b → 5a, 5c → 5a (via `ddx bead dep add`).
Parent ddx-7b753655 updated with --notes recording the decomposition.

Why this is the right cut: 5a is a self-contained backend feature
testable in isolation and unlocks the other two. 5b and 5c each
exercise a different surface (UI vs live-data integration test) and
will land independently once 5a is merged.

Implement the documented post-run merge-gate behavior in execute-bead.

## Goals
- Resolve applicable graph-authored execution documents from the governing snapshot used for the iteration
- Run the required execution documents after the agent completes and before land
- Preserve instead of land when a required execution fails or a ratchet gate blocks the iteration
- Record gate outcomes in the execute-bead result/evidence surface and through the existing exec history/runtime surfaces
- Add automated coverage for merge-by-default, required gate failure, and successful gate-passing land behavior","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"merge failed

Implement the first shipped single-project loop that continuously works one project's executable-bead queue using the explicit execute-bead contract.

## Scope
- Monitor one project queue for execution-ready beads using the documented queue-ordering contract and the generic execution validator as a gate
- Run `ddx agent execute-bead` for the selected bead from the project root; do not introduce a second worktree lifecycle around the command
- Run required post-run validity checks and execution checks before classifying the attempt
- On execution failure, validation failure, or land conflict, preserve the iteration and evidence exactly as the execute-bead contract specifies, leave the target branch untouched, record the non-landed result, and continue scanning
- On success, land by rebase plus fast-forward, reset loop state to the updated branch tip, and continue scanning for the next executable bead
- Support a minimal control surface suitable for standalone use now and ddx-server hosting later without pulling in multi-project scheduling

## Boundaries
- Single-project only for the first release
- No inline PTY policy or hidden HELIX scheduling heuristics
- Multi-project worker pools and multi-host control planes remain follow-on work","id":"ddx-7bf45ce1","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli","area:api"],"parent":"ddx-aac79489","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"implement: single-project execute-bead loop","updated_at":"2026-04-10T17:08:15.039791148Z"}

Its ID is referenced in this bead's events.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T00:56:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2278885","closing_commit_sha":"e1e5f7cfd8c9656260461a7e63d3e67883bdd77a","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:33.7239674Z","description":"## Two-layer problem

### Layer 1 (mechanical bug)

When a worker invokes `ddx bead create` from inside its execute-bead worktree, the resulting bead IDs are malformed. Reproduced today on this project:

  /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx bead ready  → 11 entries with IDs like
  `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-e630aeae`

These were the read-coverage child beads spawned from the ddx-44236615 worker (commit ebeb2464). They have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels — only the ID field is mangled. The pattern `.execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<short>-<random>` matches the worktree directory name format used by `ddx agent execute-bead`. Most likely failure path: the bead-id resolver consults `os.Getwd()` (or equivalent) to derive a project context prefix, sees the worktree dir, and uses it instead of the real project root + a fresh `ddx-<8hex>` short hash.

Concrete impact:
- 50+ char IDs are unwieldy in CLI commands (`ddx bead show <id>`, `ddx bead dep add ...`).
- Cross-project readers and the bead store assume `ddx-<8hex>` prefix matching for tooling (filtering, GraphQL adapters, MCP responses) — these long IDs may sort or filter unexpectedly.
- The IDs encode ephemeral attempt timestamps, which leaks worker-internal state into a permanent identifier.
- 12 such beads exist in the tracker as of this filing (grep `.execute-bead-wt-` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`).

### Layer 2 (design question — separate decision)

Even if the IDs were correctly formed `ddx-<8hex>`, it is not obvious that `ddx bead create` from inside an execute-bead worker is the right pattern at all. Two alternatives the design has not articulated a position on:

**(a) Append to the parent bead.** When the worker discovers sub-tasks, it appends them as structured items in the parent's description, notes, or a new `kind:discovered-subtask` event. The parent bead becomes the durable record of "what this run found"; future runs (or the operator) can decompose if/when the work warrants its own tracker entries.

**(b) Surface via the execute-bead result.** The agent emits a structured `discovered_subtasks: [...]` field on result.json; the loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

**(c) Create new beads in-band (current behavior).** The worker calls `ddx bead create` directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue if the agent over-decomposes; complicates "what was THIS run's contribution" auditing.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. The 11 read-coverage children landing as a single agent's decomposition is exactly the failure mode (c) makes easy: one analysis bead spawned 11 implementation beads in a single run, all P0, all without operator review. The work may be valid — but the pattern needs a position.

## In-scope outcomes

This bead's job is to surface both layers; the implementation work decomposes into follow-ups.

1. **Concrete fix for layer 1**: bead-id resolver should normalize worktree paths to the real project root before allocating an ID. Test: `ddx bead create` invoked with cwd=`<repo>/.execute-bead-wt-...` produces `ddx-<8hex>`, not `.execute-bead-wt-...-<random>`.
2. **Mass repair for the 12 existing malformed beads**: either rewrite their IDs to fresh `ddx-<8hex>` (with a redirect or alias map for any external references), or close them as deferred and re-file with proper IDs. Decision deferred until layer 2 is settled.
3. **Layer 2 decision**: file a separate design bead (or amendment to FEAT-006 / FEAT-010) that picks (a), (b), or (c) and documents the rationale. This bead does NOT pre-decide; it captures the question.

## In-scope files (layer 1 fix)

- `cli/cmd/bead_create.go` (or wherever bead-create resolves project context).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` or `cli/internal/bead/id.go` if ID generation lives there.
- A unit test that drops cwd into a worktree-shaped dir and asserts the resulting bead id matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`.

(Locate via: `grep -rn "execute-bead-wt\|FindProjectRoot\|projectRoot" cli/internal/bead/ cli/cmd/bead_*.go`.)

## Out of scope

- The 11 read-coverage child beads' actual implementation work — that's separate beads.
- Default behavior change for whether workers can/should create beads — that's the layer-2 design bead.
- GraphQL or MCP changes to handle long IDs — fix the source instead.

## Acceptance

Layer-1 only (this bead):

1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` passes after the fix.
2. New unit test in `cli/internal/bead/` (or `cli/cmd/`) that simulates a bead-create call from `cwd=<project>/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-XXXXX-<timestamp>-<random>` and asserts the new bead's `id` field matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`. Test fails before the fix, passes after.
3. `grep -E '"id":"\.execute-bead-wt-' .ddx/beads.jsonl` returns either 0 (mass repair) or 12 with a recorded decision in this bead's notes that the existing 12 are deferred to a follow-up bead.
4. A separate bead is filed (or referenced existing one updated) that captures the layer-2 design question (a/b/c). Reference its ID in this bead's events.

## Why one bead, not an epic

Layer 1 is a tight 1-3 file fix. Layer 2 is a design discussion that should be its own artifact, not a sibling task. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.","closing_commit_sha":"c8841f9978d5dfe595c33537ac25eebda74b3e30","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:40:27.168186276Z","description":"Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:53:52.508815722Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260502T205137-13829788","harness":"claude","input_tokens":28,"output_tokens":5675,"total_tokens":5703,"cost_usd":1.48122975,"duration_ms":132793,"exit_code":1}","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:53:52.523252789Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=5703 cost_usd=1.4812"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"exit status 1

(a) What is done

  AC #1 — Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites:
    - cli/cmd/work.go:68            (30*time.Second, with help text noting opt-out)
    - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568     (30*time.Second, same help text)
    - cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:118 (pollInterval := 30 * time.Second)
    - cli/internal/server/server.go:2328          (pollInterval := 30 * time.Second)

  AC #2 — execute_bead_loop.go: when nextCandidate returns no eligible
    bead AND poll-interval > 0, the loop now emits loop.idle, resets the
    per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps, sleeps, and continues
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:492-559). The early-return
    path only fires for the explicit opt-out (--once or
    --poll-interval=0).

  AC #3 — Routing preflight rejection is per-bead `continue`, not a loop
    exit (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:608-651). The bead is
    recorded as a worker-level failure, and the loop proceeds to the
    next candidate (still honouring --once when set).

  AC #4 — loop.end event includes a structured exit_reason field
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:432-441). Recognized values
    populated in this loop: "sigint", "sigterm", "fatal_config",
    "once_complete", "explicit_poll_zero", "context_cancelled". The
    "providers_exhausted" slot is reserved (comment at lines 428-431)
    for ddx-aede917d.

  AC #5 — Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as a transient
    "running (idle)" substate. The loop emits a loop.idle event +
    progress event with Phase="loop.idle"
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:517-533). The server
    progress consumer maps this to WorkerRecord.Substate = "idle"
    (cli/internal/server/workers.go:1360-1371) and clears it on the
    next active phase (workers.go:852, execute_bead_loop.go:564-572).
    The terminal "exited" status is reserved for true exits.

    - TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues

    - TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits

    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:247-257

  AC #7 (manual verification) — Not re-run in this pass; the behaviour

Fix execute-bead so omitted `--prompt` no longer produces an under-specified agent task.

## Required implementation
1. Replace the bare string fallback in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` with deterministic prompt synthesis driven by the bead record.
2. Build the default prompt from durable bead context:
   - bead id and title
   - description
   - acceptance criteria
   - relevant metadata such as `spec-id`, parent, labels, and any declared governing references when available
3. Materialize that synthesized prompt as a prompt file in the managed execution worktree instead of relying on shell-fragile inline quoting.
4. Keep `--prompt` as an explicit override; only synthesize the default prompt when the caller did not supply one.
5. Make `execute-loop` inherit the same behavior automatically by continuing to call `execute-bead` without needing its own prompt-building fork.
6. Add regression coverage proving the default path no longer sends only `Work on bead <id>.` and that the runner receives the synthesized bead context.

## Boundaries
- Do not invent a new bead schema.
- Do not move prompt assembly into HELIX-specific logic.
- Use DDx-owned bead/store/docgraph context only.","id":"ddx-e07fb546","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:bead","area:cli"],"notes":"Implemented. buildPrompt() synthesizes from bead title/description/acceptance/governing refs when --prompt is omitted. Failing citations block the PR.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T06:37:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"790f3b9752c1b3b289fd8f3725291fd5b16ed067","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:06:41.260303435Z","description":"## Problem

No single document traces the provider/model/harness resolution path from CLI flags to the final HTTP request. SD-015 and SD-023 are SPECS — they describe what should happen in 5 routing modes. The code lives across routing.go, discovery.go, agent_runner.go, runner.go, and agent_cmd.go. A newcomer debugging a routing bug must infer the flow by reading all of them.

The audit for epic ddx-2d974641 turned up a RouteRequest struct missing its Provider field, and a parallel RunOptions.Provider path that bypasses routing — neither of which is documented. This is the kind of hidden structural defect a Resolution Path document would have flagged on first read.

## Fix

Write docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (or similar location; coordinate with SD-015's author). The document traces one concrete invocation end-to-end:

    ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6

Walk through every layer, with file:line citations:

1. **CLI parse** — cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go flag extraction (line references).

2. **RouteFlags -> RouteRequest normalization** — routing.go:NormalizeRouteRequest. What each CLI flag maps to; which flags are discarded when they don't match an expected shape; the current gap around --provider.

3. **Discovery probe** — discovery.go:DiscoverProviderModels. When it fires; what it caches; the 30s TTL; how failures are handled.

4. **Candidate planning** — routing.go:BuildCandidatePlans / evaluateCandidate. For each registered harness, the state map lookup and the viability checks.

5. **Fuzzy matching** — discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel. Pool selection (global vs per-provider), normalization (current gaps), prefix + tiebreak algorithm.

6. **Scoring** — routing.go:scoreCandidate. Per-profile scoring, cost class, historical success rate, provider-affinity bonus (when implemented).

7. **Candidate ranking** — routing.go:RankCandidates / SelectBestCandidate. Non-viable rejection, stable tiebreak.

8. **Dispatch** — runner.go:Run. Harness-specific branches (virtual, agent, script, HTTP-provider-embedded, default exec). For embedded: agent_runner.go:RunAgent -> resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider -> agent library call.

9. **Wire-level request** — ddx-agent builds the HTTP body (system_prompt + messages + tools), sends to the provider. What the server receives.

10. **Response parsing** — streaming or non-streaming decode; where 'unexpected end of JSON input' (ddx-6a5dfe35) surfaces.

Include a SEQUENCE DIAGRAM (mermaid or ASCII) showing the layers.

Include a GAP TABLE at the top listing every currently-missing piece (as of whatever the SD-015 implementation state is) with links to the specific beads tracking each gap.

Keep the document MAINTAINED alongside the code: update it when RouteRequest gains a field, when a new routing mode lands, when fuzzy match gains a normalization step.

## Files likely touched

- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md (new)
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md — add a single-line pointer at the top of the 'Overview' section
- skills/ddx/reference/agents.md — add a one-line pointer in 'Harness / profile / persona dispatch' section so agents consulting the skill can follow up to the full trace when needed

## Out of scope

- Rewriting SD-015 or SD-023 — this doc complements them; they describe intent, this describes current reality.
- Documenting future routing modes that haven't When invoked with\n# --strict, also verifies the line contains a Go symbol hint when the path\n# looks like Go source.\n#\n# Cited as the CI step that prevents SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md from\n# rotting silently as routing.go / discovery.go / types.go evolve.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nDOC_PATH=\"${1:-docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md}\"\n\nif [ ! -f \"$DOC_PATH\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $DOC_PATH not found\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\n# Match citations of the form `path/to/file.ext:NN` inside backticks.\n# We only validate citations whose path looks like a file (contains a slash\n# and ends with a known extension).\ncitations=$(grep -oE '`[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\\.(go|ts|tsx|js|yaml|yml|md|sh):[0-9]+`' \"$DOC_PATH\" | tr -d '`' | sort -u)\n\nif [ -z \"$citations\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: no citations found in $DOC_PATH — fail-closed\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nfailures=0\ntotal=0\n\nwhile IFS= read -r cite; do\n    [ -z \"$cite\" ] && continue\n    total=$((total + 1))\n    path=\"${cite%:*}\"\n    line=\"${cite##*:}\"\n    if [ ! -f \"$path\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: MISSING FILE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Line count check; awk 'END{print NR}' counts lines portably.\n    nlines=$(awk 'END{print NR}' \"$path\")\n    if [ \"$line\" -gt \"$nlines\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: LINE OUT OF RANGE: $cite (file has $nlines lines)\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Sanity check: the line must not be blank, since every real citation\n    # points at a struct, func, or statement.\n    content=$(awk -v n=\"$line\" 'NR==n' \"$path\")\n    if [ -z \"$(echo \"$content\" | tr -d '[:space:]')\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: BLANK LINE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\ndone <<< \"$citations\"\n\nif [ \"$failures\" -gt 0 ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $failures/$total failed for $DOC_PATH\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"citations: $total/$total ok for $DOC_PATH\"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"465c02eb-3d0f-4e0e-9149-057b595f6ab5","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:07.386Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md\n# points to a file that exists and a line that exists. If flags.Profile is set → Profile.\n287\t//  4. Else fall back to cfg.Model (same catalog resolution) or cfg.Profile.\n288\t//  5. If nothing is set → cfg.Harness becomes HarnessOverride (legacy fallback).\n289\tfunc NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest {\n290\t\tif catalog == nil {\n291\t\t\tcatalog = BuiltinCatalog\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\treq := RouteRequest{\n295\t\t\tEffort:      flags.Effort,\n296\t\t\tPermissions: flags.Permissions,\n297\t\t}\n298\t\n299\t\t// 1. Harness override constrains routing to one harness.\n300\t\t//    --provider that names a known harness (or alias like \"local\") also\n301\t\t//    pins the harness so the routing engine never falls through to a\n302\t\t//    different harness.\n303\t\tif flags.Harness != \"\" {\n304\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = flags.Harness\n305\t\t} else if flags.Provider != \"\" {\n306\t\t\tresolved := resolveHarnessAlias(flags.Provider)\n307\t\t\t// Only treat as harness override if it names a registered harness;\n308\t\t\t// otherwise it's a ddx-agent provider name (e.g. \"vidar\") and\n309\t\t\t// should not constrain harness selection.\n310\t\t\tif _, ok := builtinHarnesses[resolved]; ok {\n311\t\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = resolved"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"3708917d-10f5-4047-87a8-eca15bf792b1","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:52.477Z"}

Returns\n65\t// empty string if no match found.\n66\tfunc (d *DiscoveryResult) FuzzyMatchModel(input string) (resolved string, fuzzy bool) {\n67\t\tif d == nil {\n68\t\t\treturn \"\", false\n69\t\t}\n70\t\treturn fuzzyMatchInPool(input, d.AllModels())\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t// fuzzyMatchInPool resolves input against a pool of model IDs."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"21b0e478-edd0-4d23-81e6-41a9cd63003c","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:53.316Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1065:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go (or equivalent) declares an 'omlx' surface with at least the six canonical refs listed above mapping to their server-side IDs on vidar-omlx.

2. Catalog lookup is case-insensitive for the omlx surface (matches per v0.3.11 LookupModelLimits requirement).

3. Unit test in cli/internal/agent/ asserts that for at least 'qwen/qwen3.6' and 'qwen/qwen3.5', catalog.ContextWindowForModel() and the surface lookup return the omlx concrete ID (not the 'embedded-openai' lmstudio-shaped ID).

4. Canonical refs with no omlx target are rejected at route-plan time with a clear diagnostic ('ref X has no omlx target; vidar-omlx offers: [list]'), not a downstream 404 from the server.

5. With the catalog change, 'ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6' resolves to Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit at the routing layer (the downstream dispatch may still hit the parse bug — fixing that is ddx-6a5dfe35's concern).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:57:31.967128605Z","description":"## Superseded — wrong diagnosis

Filed under the belief that DDx needs hand-authored omlx surface entries in the model catalog to map qwen/qwen3.6 -> Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit on vidar-omlx.

**This is not the right fix.** An audit of the resolution path (see parent epic ddx-2d974641) showed that the real defect is in fuzzy matching + the RouteRequest plumbing: the fuzzy matcher exists (discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel) and provider discovery exists (DiscoverProviderModels probes /v1/models on each provider), but:

1. The matcher uses STRICT case-sensitive prefix — qwen/qwen3.6 can't match Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit even semantically.
2. The matcher runs over a provider-union (d.AllModels()) that loses provider identity.
3. --provider is silently discarded at NormalizeRouteRequest (types.go:330 RouteRequest has no Provider field).
4. Catalog-resolved refs (ModelRef path) don't go through the fuzzy matcher at all.

Once those are fixed, no catalog entries are needed — canonical refs fuzzy-match against discovered omlx model IDs at runtime. See sibling beads filed under ddx-2d974641.

Closing as invalid: catalog-first approach would require hand-authoring entries for every new MLX model name format that appears, which defeats the purpose of discovery.","id":"ddx-e59e7fe8","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:catalog"],"parent":"ddx-2d974641","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"catalog: map shipped canonical model refs to omlx-flavor server IDs","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:04:35.900603126Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1083:{"acceptance":"1. Fixture-based test: 2 workers + server up + restart server mid-flight; assert workers continue + reconnect + backfill in <60s.
2. Same scenario with NO server; assert identical bead outcomes.
3. Operator-cancel test: cancel issued; preserved_for_review reported within 30s with reason operator_cancel.
4. LIVE OBSERVABILITY: start a real ddx work worker against fixture repo with server running; observe via workers panel that worker appears within 30s, freshness state transitions to 'connected', current_bead updates within 60s of bead pickup. Kill worker process; observe transition to 'disconnected' within 2× probe interval.
5. Soak: ≥7 days of operator use without manual intervention; summary at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/soak-summary.md.
6. SOAK ABORT CRITERIA: rollback if any of: (a) any worker's mirror_failures_count exceeds 5 in a 24h window; (b) any silent dropped backfill (had_dropped_backfill flag set on any worker); (c) any operator-initiated SIGTERM of a worker process during soak (workers should not need killing); (d) any bead requires manual git-reset to recover; (e) any 'preserved_orphaned' outcome on a bead the worker actually finished. If any of these fire during soak, file a regression bead, halt the soak clock, and resume after fix lands.
7. ADR-022 step 7 requires migrating from in-process agent execution to a server-spawn model (workers spawned by the server, not by the CLI directly). The current execute-loop launch path uses cli/internal/server/workers.go handleStartExecuteLoopWorker to start a worker subprocess, but this handler may not properly validate all execution parameters or follow the unified ExecuteLoopSpec lifecycle (ApplyDefaults → Validate → StartExecuteLoop).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (exact line to be confirmed after ddx-89a9c305 lands): the current server-spawn path constructs CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime manually rather than going through ApplyDefaults → Validate → StartExecuteLoop with a unified spec.
- triage:needs-investigation label was added because the exact wiring was unclear at filing time. Investigation findings: the server-spawn handler calls StartExecuteLoop via the workers subsystem, but intermediate struct construction (before ddx-89a9c305 unifies the spec) may drop fields.
- The server binary is the one that actually execs 'ddx work'; the subprocess inherits from server-resolved settings, not from a fully validated ExecuteLoopSpec.

PROPOSED FIX
- Confirm handleStartExecuteLoopWorker in cli/internal/server/workers.go validates through ApplyDefaults → Validate before calling StartExecuteLoop.
- If gaps exist: wire the unified ExecuteLoopSpec (from ddx-89a9c305) into the spawn path.
- Verify the spawned 'ddx work' subprocess receives all spec fields (MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, FromRev) via the exec args or a spec file.

NON-SCOPE
- Changes to the exec'd ddx work subprocess behavior (that's C10 / ddx-f9acb86d).
- REST/GraphQL request decoding (those are ddx-da9e9491 and ddx-ce1d6309).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"detail":"routing preflight rejected (harness=codex model=): no viable routing candidate ## Why this attempt declined to commit

The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.

### Actual surface (vs stated estimate)

cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:

- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction
- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization
- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer`
- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation)
- in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService)
- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net
- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress`
- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS
- terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result
- BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes

Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:

- /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress
- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial)
- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging
- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent

### Test surface

cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:

- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or
- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).

This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates."

### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)

"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit"

Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper.

### Size-check verdict

- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.
- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).
- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.

The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.

## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)

Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):

### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing
Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)
Scope:
- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).
- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.
- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.
- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.
- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.
- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.
Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.
Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.

### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion
Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec
Scope:
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.
- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.
- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.
Estimated: ~300–500 LOC.

### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)
Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit
Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:
- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.
- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.
- POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once.
- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).
- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.
Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.
Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.

### Dependencies
- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)
- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)

## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit

The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.

## Bookkeeping

A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1121:{"acceptance":"FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 define a structured embedded-agent progress contract for managed runs; the contract names the required phases and fields, distinguishes runtime-only progress from tracked execution evidence, and defines one shared read model for CLI and server/UI consumers","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T05:09:51Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"82ff773b8d56e7a883dd79ba7cd8ad963c322439","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:16:04.952097087Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f5337613","depends_on_id":"ddx-ffda4fb5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:20:18Z"}],"description":"<context-digest>
Review area: embedded ddx-agent runtime observability and live worker progress. Evidence covers FEAT-006 execution/evidence ownership, FEAT-002 server activity surfaces, FEAT-008 web UI expectations, execute-loop worker supervision, and the need for DDx-owned progress telemetry beyond coarse session logs when DDx runs the embedded harness.
</context-digest>
Define the progress-event contract for managed embedded-agent runs so DDx can surface meaningful live execution status in the CLI and server/UI.

## Goals
- Define structured phase/heartbeat output for embedded-agent runs (queueing, launch, running, post-checks, landing, preserved, done)
- Define the minimal per-event fields needed for operator visibility and later reporting: worker/project/bead/run identity, phase, elapsed/heartbeat timing, harness/provider/model/profile, and token/cost deltas when available
- Clarify which live progress artifacts remain runtime-only and which summaries/indexes are exposed through DDx-owned APIs and UI surfaces
- Keep provider-native transcript ownership external; DDx progress output should be normalized runtime telemetry, not transcript duplication

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006, FEAT-002, and FEAT-008 so embedded-agent progress visibility is an explicit contract rather than an implementation detail
- Clarify how progress events relate to tracked execution artifacts, session logs, and server-managed worker state

## Required implementation planning
- Define the server/API/UI read model for live worker progress and recent phase history
- Define how CLI and UI consume the same structured progress feed without introducing hidden workflow policy","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"checkpointing dirty worktree: git stash: error: could not write index
