cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! Adds tables for:\\n4\\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\\n5\\t//! - users: global user identities\\n6\\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\\n7\\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\\n8\\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\\n9\\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\\n10\\t//!\\n11\\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\\n12\\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\\n13\\t\\n14\\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\\n15\\t///\\n16\\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\\n17\\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\\n18\\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t    // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t        )\\\",\\n29\\t    )\\n30\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenants table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n31\\t\\n32\\t    // Create users table.\\n33\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n34\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\\n35\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n36\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n37\\t            email              TEXT,\\n38\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n39\\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\\n40\\t        )\\\",\\n41\\t    )\\n42\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n43\\t\\n44\\t    // Create user_identities table.\\n45\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t        )\\\",\\n54\\t    )\\n55\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t    // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t        )\\\",\\n67\\t    )\\n68\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n74\\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\\n75\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n76\\t            PRIMARY KEY Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod .ddx/beads.jsonl:1019:{"acceptance":"- [ ] execute-loop with zero flags escalates across cheap → standard → smart on failure\n- [ ] Review agent auto-selects a tier \u003e= impl tier, defaulting to smart\n- [ ] Provider health probe runs at attempt time; unhealthy providers skip cleanly with cooldown\n- [ ] Each attempt records tier + provider + probe_result + outcome on the bead\n- [ ] Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible\n- [ ] --min-tier / --max-tier overrides work and are documented\n- [ ] --harness / --model debug overrides disable escalation\n- [ ] Zero-flag 'ddx agent execute-loop' is the documented happy path","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c84e9e6fc5e69cf00878adc228f621dedc22ea83","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.621451901Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-a317208c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-44084b3e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-5bac3c6b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:57:36Z"}],"description":"The CLI should not require the user to enumerate harness/model pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.\n\n## Principle\n\nDiscovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.\n\n## Default policy (no flags)\n\n- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead\n- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close\n- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry\n- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)\n- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.\n\n## Resource discovery at attempt time\n\n- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)\n- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier\n- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate\n- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)\n\n## Rare overrides\n\n- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)\n- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)\n- --harness \u003cname\u003e / --model \u003cname\u003e (debug override; disables escalation)\n\n## Surface\n\nDefault invocation becomes:\n\n  ddx agent execute-loop --once\n\nEverything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.\n\n## Events\n\nEach attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop:\n- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.\n- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.\n- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.\n\n## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)","events":[{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"This bead is one of the review-loop primitives the meta-epic depends on. See ddx-4b59b816 for the full scope.","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:47:43.087833799Z","kind":"note","source":"cost-tiered work meta-epic cross-link 2026-04-15","summary":"Part of cost-tiered execution + measurement meta-epic ddx-4b59b816 — maximize closed-bead throughput per dollar."},{"actor":"ddx","body":"merge conflict\npreserve_ref=refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-dc231144/20260415T055750-15712870-7d18cb4a228d\nresult_rev=92d10ad16da5479a35882850ccc8303d3d9a970b\nbase_rev=b8c2395c8fb2885c140e4d1699a9815700fe2f17","created_at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35.563337116Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"land_conflict"}],"id":"ddx-dc231144","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"notes":"\u003coperator-feedback 2026-04-15\u003e\nAdditional observation: --local is too prominent as a flag. The default IS server-worker mode, but I (an experienced DDx operator) still reached for --local reflexively because I wanted a synchronous handle to the subprocess. The root cause: I didn't know how to tail a server-side worker from the CLI.\n\nPossible fixes (minor, could be part of this bead's scope):\n- 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --follow' that submits to server and tails the worker log to stdout until completion, then returns. Gives the synchronous-feel without forking a subprocess.\n- 'ddx server workers wait \u003cworker-id\u003e' that blocks until the named worker is in a terminal state.\n- Document in execute-loop --help that --local is for debugging only; the primary surface is server-worker.\n\nNot worth a separate bead — just ergonomics for the already-correct default.\n\u003c/operator-feedback\u003e","parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:44:11.543185612Z"}

These shims prevent callers from adopting the canonical names and leave dead identifier surface that grep/tooling cannot distinguish from active usage.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nC13 (ddx-387a0178) introduces the canonical type names as part of the file/identifier rename step and adds shim typedefs for backward compat during the migration window. The shim typedefs live in the files renamed by C13 (exact file:line determined after C13 lands). The CLI aliases ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead continue to register live cobra commands after C13, pointing at logic now accessible via ddx work and ddx try.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Sweep all callsites off the shim names (grep for ExecuteBeadWorker | ExecuteBeadReport | ExecuteBeadResult | ExecuteBead\\b | ExecuteBeadStatus | ExecuteBeadOutcome in cli/).\n- Delete the shim typedefs from C13.\n- Remove cobra command registrations for ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead; replace with a cobra RunE that prints a deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.\n- Verify: grep returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test files.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Deleting the entire ddx agent command group (that is ddx-d8474e0e, a separate bead).\n- Changing execute-loop/try behavior.","id":"ddx-ddacd4ff","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:cleanup"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"C14: delete ExecuteBead* shims and aliases; verify all callers ported","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:09:43.594638161Z"}

AC #4 (\"CI runs the suite for both backends\") requires a\n     running axon-server (per TD-030 §D1 / §D4 the axon backend\n     refuses all ops when axon is unreachable). No such server is\n     available in this execution environment, and there is no\n     vendored test harness or \"skip if AXON_ADDR unset\" gate yet.\n\n  Implementing this bead now would require either:\n    (a) reproducing the axon backend, the interface refactor, the\n        gRPC client generation, and a test harness in a single\n        execute-bead pass — far outside the scope this bead was\n        sized for, and explicitly the work of three other beads;\n    (b) committing red or stub code (parameterized harness\n        targeting a non-existent backend) — which the execute-bead\n        contract forbids (\"Do not commit partial, exploratory, or\n        red code hoping the reviewer will accept it\").\n\nwhat a follow-up attempt would need:\n  - ddx-bbdd7564 closes with the TD-030-shaped per-entity Backend\n    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to\n    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the\n    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the\n    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).\n  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in\n    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can\n    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a\n    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit\n    \"skip if AXON_ADDR unset\" gate consistent with the project's\n    test-tier conventions).\n  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for\n    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256\n    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree\n    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.\n  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a\n    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for \"jsonl\" and \"axon\"\n    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention\n    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),\n    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both\n    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new\n    contention scenario surfaces.\n\n  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead\n  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564\n  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual \"Depends on …\"\n  language in the description is not enough; a concrete\n  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for\n  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that\n  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.\nresult_rev=22166effd010cb59f29f3ea4c21bd2faf154a229\nbase_rev=22166effd010cb59f29f3ea4c21bd2faf154a229","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:49:30.323905511Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"already_satisfied"}],"execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-dfb1c6d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2"," area:beads"," area:storage"," kind:test"," backend-migration"],"parent":"ddx-5d49b14e","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-0a0f70a7","status":"closed","title":"chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend","updated_at":"2026-05-03T02:49:30.34811111Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1040:{"acceptance":"Regression test in cli/internal/server/workers_test.go: starts a WorkerManager against a temp project (real git repo fixture), seeds one open bead, runs a worker whose agent-run produces a single commit, waits for runWorker to exit, asserts (a) git log on the project's target branch shows the worker's commit SHA, (b) the WorkerRecord.LastResult.ResultRev matches the SHA visible in git log, (c) ddx bead show returns status=closed with the commit SHA recorded as the closing session. Test must fail pre-fix and pass post-fix. The workers.go:runWorker closure at line 248 calls LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult before returning from the executor. `ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model sonnet` (no --local) run against the ddx repo with one ready bead advances main by at least one commit, visible via git log main -1.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:36:47.352994047Z","description":"Server-managed execute-loop workers produce commits that never reach the target branch. This is the default path when an operator runs `ddx agent execute-loop` without `--local`, yet the commits are silently lost.\n\n## Evidence\n\ncli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker (function at line ~237) builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc closure at line 248:\n\n    executor := agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {\n        runner := m.buildAgentRunner(m.projectRoot)\n        gitOps := \u0026agent.RealGitOps{}\n\n        res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(m.projectRoot, beadID, agent.ExecuteBeadOptions{\n            Harness: spec.Harness,\n            Model:   spec.Model,\n            Effort:  spec.Effort,\n        }, gitOps, runner)\n        if err != nil {\n            return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err\n        }\n        return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{...}, nil\n    })\n\nThis closure calls agent.ExecuteBead directly and returns the ExecuteBeadReport with ResultRev populated — but never calls agent.LandBeadResult. The commit sits in the worktree's branch; nothing advances the project's target branch.\n\nGrep confirms LandBeadResult has exactly two production call sites in the entire repo:\n\n    cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1372   — inside runAgentExecuteLoop's --local branch\n    cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:134  — interactive `ddx agent execute-bead` CLI\n\nNeither is reachable from cli/internal/server/workers.go.\n\n## Why this matters\n\nUnder the current help text, `ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model sonnet` (no --local) is the default and submits to the server. Per the help:\n\n    --local    Run inline in current process instead of server worker\n               (default: submit to server)\n\nEvery operator who follows the documented default pattern is running workers whose commits will not land. The loop reports ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess to the tracker (CloseWithEvidence fires), closes the bead, and writes tracker events — but `git log main` never sees the commit.\n\n## Scope of this bead\n\nThis bead is a P0 CALL-SITE FIX. It does NOT redesign the merge orchestration — that is covered by ddx-8746d8a6 (the meta-bead). This bead closes the acute correctness gap: ensure the server path, today, lands commits the same way the --local path does, so work stops being silently dropped while the meta redesign is in flight.\n\n## The fix (interim, pre-meta)\n\nMirror the --local closure's land sequence into workers.go:248. After agent.ExecuteBead returns a non-nil result, call agent.LandBeadResult + agent.ApplyLandingToResult before returning the ExecuteBeadReport. Pseudocode:\n\n    res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(...)\n    if err != nil \u0026\u0026 res == nil {\n        return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err\n    }\n    if res != nil {\n        if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(\n            m.projectRoot, res, \u0026agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}, agent.BeadLandingOptions{},\n        ); landErr == nil {\n            agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)\n        } else if err == nil {\n            err = landErr\n        }\n    }\n    if err != nil {\n        return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err\n    }\n    return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{...populated from res...}, nil\n\nThis IS the same merge-instead-of-rebase pattern the meta redesign will replace — but it at least stops losing work today. The meta redesign (ddx-8746d8a6) will rewrite this call site entirely, so consider this a stopgap until the coordinator goroutine is in place.\n\n## Regression test\n\ncli/internal/server/workers_test.go: start a WorkerManager against a temp project, submit a worker that produces a single commit, wait for the worker to finish, assert git log on the temp project's target branch shows the worker's commit is in the history and referenced by the worker's LastResult.ResultRev. The test must fail against the current code (commit is NOT in main's history post-worker-exit) and pass after the fix.\n\n## Dependency\n\nDo NOT merge this fix onto main until the meta-bead (ddx-8746d8a6) design is approved. The stopgap uses the flawed `git merge --no-edit` Merge() path and will produce 2-parent merge commits. The alternative is to wait for the meta redesign's Land() coordinator and skip the interim. The tradeoff:\n\n- Land interim fix now: stops silent work loss, accepts ongoing merge-commit noise until the meta redesign.\n- Skip interim fix: silent work loss continues (every server-worker bead's commits never reach main), but the main branch history stays cleaner.\n\nRecommendation: DO land the interim fix. Silent work loss is worse than merge-commit noise because it destroys the worker's output. Merge-commit noise is cosmetic and gets flattened by the meta redesign anyway.","id":"ddx-e14efc58","issue_type":"bug","parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"P0 bug: server runWorker never calls LandBeadResult; commits silently lost","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.485315837Z"}

TestAttestation_UntrustedHub_Rejects verifies untrusted hub returns 403.\n7. TestWakeOnApprove_RoutesToOwningSpoke verifies wake signal reaches correct spoke.\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:05:44.369592605Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-e2c217d1","depends_on_id":"ddx-f171a715","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:05:44Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-e2c217d1","depends_on_id":"ddx-0793ac75","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:05:44Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nBLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement in coordinator topology. When the hub forwards a mutation to a spoke, there is no signed attestation of origin identity, and the spoke cannot distinguish a legitimate coordinator-forwarded request from a hub-originated forgery. Without dual authorization on both origin and coordinator identity, any compromised hub can submit mutations on behalf of any operator.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: no attestation metadata structure defined; no signing of identity claims.\n- cli/internal/server/server.go: requireTrusted checks only immediate transport peer (hub); does not validate origin identity independently.\n- wake-on-approve routing (the 'wake-on-approve targets owning client node's loop' requirement) has no implementation path in the current federation layer.\n- ddx-f171a715 (federation foundation, a dep) and ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Define attestation metadata structure: signed structured metadata carrying origin identity (WhoIs peer at coordinator edge) and coordinator identity (hub's own attested ID). Signing mechanism: HMAC or similar per plan-locked decisions.\n- Spoke validates BOTH: origin identity (verified against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy) AND hub identity (verified against registered federation peers).\n- wake-on-approve: when spoke receives an approve mutation, it routes wake signal to the owning client node's execute-loop (project-ownership authoritative on the spoke).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Transport-level encryption (handled by tailnet).\n- Key management or certificate infrastructure beyond HMAC shared secret.\n- Story 14 query fan-out behavior.","id":"ddx-e2c217d1","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:15","area:server","kind:feature","blocks-remote"],"parent":"ddx-1d85c927","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"operator-prompts: multi-node trust attestation forwarding (gates non-localhost write)","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:24:30.279838991Z"}

Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) and [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md).\\n\\nSD-015 and SD-023 describe *intent* — what the routing layer should do across\\nfive routing modes. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one\\ndocument instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`,\\n`discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`.\\n\\n**Author's invocation used throughout this trace:**\\n\\n```bash\\nddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\\n```\\n\\n**Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).**\\nLine numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section\\nat the end for how this document is kept in sync.\\n\\n## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18)\\n\\nEach gap links to its tracking bead. parse\\n\\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\\nfrom the inner command.\\n\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\\n  are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\\n  `effort`, `local`).\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\\n  it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\\n  runs before any bead is claimed.\\n\\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\\n`RunOptions`:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\\n  constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\\n\\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\\n\\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\\n\\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \\\"\\\"` branch assigns\\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \\\"\\\"` branch: the\\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\\n  value is silently discarded.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \\\"\\\"` branch routes the\\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\\n`{ModelPin: \\\"qwen/qwen3.6\\\"}`.\\n\\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\\n  way to force a re-probe in tests.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\\nthe catalog:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\\n  discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \\\"\\\" \u0026\u0026 req.ModelRef == \\\"\\\" \u0026\u0026 r.Discovery == nil`.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\\n  `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\\n  the requested model.\\n\\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\\n  viability gate. Checks:\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\\n\\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\\nlevel support. That is Gap #6.\\n\\n## Layer 5 — Fuzzy matching\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:66` — `FuzzyMatchModel(input)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:70` — delegates to `fuzzyMatchInPool` using\\n  `d.AllModels()` (a flat deduplicated pool across all providers — not per-provider).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` — `fuzzyMatchInPool(input, pool)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:78` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:82` — exact-match short-circuit.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:89` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:96` — prefix match: candidate `m` is considered if `len(m) \u003e len(input) \u0026\u0026 m[:len(input)] == input`. **The comparison is case-sensitive and treats slashes literally (Gap #3).**\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:106` — tiebreak: shortest suffix wins; alphabetical on equal suffix length. **Not version-aware (Gap #5).**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\nPool likely contains `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Input `qwen/qwen3.6` fails the\\nexact check, then fails the prefix check because `Q != q` and the slash is not\\nin the pool entries. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1065:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go (or equivalent) declares an 'omlx' surface with at least the six canonical refs listed above mapping to their server-side IDs on vidar-omlx.\n\n2. Catalog lookup is case-insensitive for the omlx surface (matches per v0.3.11 LookupModelLimits requirement).\n\n3. Unit test in cli/internal/agent/ asserts that for at least 'qwen/qwen3.6' and 'qwen/qwen3.5', catalog.ContextWindowForModel() and the surface lookup return the omlx concrete ID (not the 'embedded-openai' lmstudio-shaped ID).\n\n4. Canonical refs with no omlx target are rejected at route-plan time with a clear diagnostic ('ref X has no omlx target; vidar-omlx offers: [list]'), not a downstream 404 from the server.\n\n5. With the catalog change, 'ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6' resolves to Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit at the routing layer (the downstream dispatch may still hit the parse bug — fixing that is ddx-6a5dfe35's concern).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:57:31.967128605Z","description":"## Superseded — wrong diagnosis\n\nFiled under the belief that DDx needs hand-authored omlx surface entries in the model catalog to map qwen/qwen3.6 -\u003e Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit on vidar-omlx.\n\n**This is not the right fix.** An audit of the resolution path (see parent epic ddx-2d974641) showed that the real defect is in fuzzy matching + the RouteRequest plumbing: the fuzzy matcher exists (discovery.go:FuzzyMatchModel) and provider discovery exists (DiscoverProviderModels probes /v1/models on each provider), but:\n\n1. The matcher uses STRICT case-sensitive prefix — qwen/qwen3.6 can't match Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit even semantically.\n2. The matcher runs over a provider-union (d.AllModels()) that loses provider identity.\n3. --provider is silently discarded at NormalizeRouteRequest (types.go:330 RouteRequest has no Provider field).\n4. Catalog-resolved refs (ModelRef path) don't go through the fuzzy matcher at all.\n\nOnce those are fixed, no catalog entries are needed — canonical refs fuzzy-match against discovered omlx model IDs at runtime. See sibling beads filed under ddx-2d974641.\n\nClosing as invalid: catalog-first approach would require hand-authoring entries for every new MLX model name format that appears, which defeats the purpose of discovery.","id":"ddx-e59e7fe8","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:catalog"],"parent":"ddx-2d974641","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"catalog: map shipped canonical model refs to omlx-flavor server IDs","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:04:35.900603126Z"}

Missing image-generation skill. The `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` skill is\n   referenced from `.crush/skills/` and `.pi/skills/` as symlinks pointing to\n   `.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`, but that target directory does\n   not exist in this worktree (only helix-* skills and `ddx` are under\n   `.agents/skills/`). Without the skill the generator cannot run.\n2. No execution definition registered. `ddx exec list --json` returns `[]`;\n   there is no nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator definition, and no\n   definition lists `principles-composite` (or any visual artifact id) under\n   produces_artifact. `ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite` therefore\n   fails with \"artifact \\\"principles-composite\\\" not found in document graph\"\n   — the artifact is not registered, and even if it were, selectArtifactGenerator\n   in cli/cmd/artifact.go:99 would error with \"no generator definition or\n   generated_by provenance\".\n3. Sidecar metadata not authored. The plan calls for\n   `principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}` co-located with the V5\n   prompts, with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges. The `.ddx.yaml` sidecar\n   for the (yet-unrendered) composite cannot be written without a real\n   `generated_by` run record, which only the substrate run produces.\n\nWhat a follow-up attempt would need (in order)\n1. A predecessor bead that installs / vendors the `nano-banana-pro-openrouter`\n   skill into `.agents/skills/` so the broken symlinks resolve. LIVE OBSERVABILITY: start a real ddx work worker against fixture repo with server running; observe via workers panel that worker appears within 30s, freshness state transitions to 'connected', current_bead updates within 60s of bead pickup. Kill worker process; observe transition to 'disconnected' within 2× probe interval.\n5. Soak: ≥7 days of operator use without manual intervention; summary at .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/soak-summary.md.\n6. SOAK ABORT CRITERIA: rollback if any of: (a) any worker's mirror_failures_count exceeds 5 in a 24h window; (b) any silent dropped backfill (had_dropped_backfill flag set on any worker); (c) any operator-initiated SIGTERM of a worker process during soak (workers should not need killing); (d) any bead requires manual git-reset to recover; (e) any 'preserved_orphaned' outcome on a bead the worker actually finished. If any of these fire during soak, file a regression bead, halt the soak clock, and resume after fix lands.\n7. No behavior change to existing query fan-out tests.\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T23:07:45.069155129Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-eb75a32d","depends_on_id":"ddx-256af8b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T23:08:10Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nFederation today supports query fan-out only (cli/internal/federation/fanout.go). For the multi-node operator-prompt path, the coordinator (hub) must route a mutation to a single spoke that owns the project, NOT fan out. No mutation routing envelope, no project-based spoke selection, and no forwarding client exist.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: FanOut function sends read queries to all spokes; has no concept of routing to a single owning spoke for mutations.\n- FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012): stores spoke records by node_id but no lookup-by-project-id helper exists.\n- No HTTP client method ForwardMutation exists in the federation package.\n- Plan-locked decision (point 6 from story-15 planning): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations; mutation envelope is HTTP POST to spoke /graphql with X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers preserved verbatim.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry FederationRegistry, projectID string) (*SpokeRecord, error): looks up the spoke that owns the project; returns error when no owner registered.\n- Add federation client method ForwardMutation(ctx context.Context, target *SpokeRecord, body []byte, headers map[string]string) (*http.Response, error): issues HTTP POST to \u003cspoke\u003e/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.\n- Add hub HTTP handler that accepts forwarding requests and routes by project (the handler that S15-7c and S15-7d build on top of).\n- Identity header values are NOT populated in this bead (that is S15-7c / ddx-ba31fec8); this bead lays the envelope and routing.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Dual authorization on the spoke side (ddx-ba31fec8).\n- Identity attestation structure (ddx-e2c217d1).\n- Changing existing query fan-out behavior.","id":"ddx-eb75a32d","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:15","kind:multi-node","area:federation"],"parent":"ddx-256af8b5","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation)","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:25:18.336732779Z"}

Three of the four are blocked on a\nprerequisite that has not landed:\n\n(a) \"Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands\"\n    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/\n    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)\n\n(c) \"Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)\"\n\nBoth presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`\n— i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and\nplan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.\n\nThe CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this\nas a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23–89):\n\n    \u003e DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current\n    \u003e contract.\n    \u003e\n    \u003e The plan's \"delete with prejudice\" list (agent_list,\n    \u003e agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,\n    \u003e agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,\n    \u003e agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands\n    \u003e inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven\n    \u003e branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume\n    \u003e programmatically.\n    \u003e\n    \u003e Plan bead #6 (\"open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`\n    \u003e if #5 surfaces gaps\") is **required, not conditional**, because\n    \u003e of this gap.\n\nConcretely:\n\n  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123–141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted\n    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,\n    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,\n    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,\n    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,\n    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,\n    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`\n    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave\n    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.\n  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires\n    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`\n    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment\n    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,\n    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).\n    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.\n\n(b) \"Migrate DDx-only subcommands\"\n    (agent_executions → tries_*; agent_log → runs_log;\n     agent_metrics → runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;\n     agent_workers → work_workers)\n\nThis portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the\nplan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — with #15\nlanding in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,\n`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue\nyet in command_factory_commands.go. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class\n   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the\n   plan).\n3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons\n   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then\n   ported in this bead.\n\nOnce those are in, the present bead becomes:\n\n  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`\n    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,\n    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,\n    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for\n    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).\n  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,\n    agent_route_status.go (the only \"delete with prejudice\" files\n    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/\n    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions\n    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go\n    rewrite).\n  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →\n    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);\n    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`\n    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`\n    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave\n    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.\n  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires\n    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`\n    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment\n    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,\n    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).\n    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.\n\n(b) \"Migrate DDx-only subcommands\"\n    (agent_executions → tries_*; agent_log → runs_log;\n     agent_metrics → runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;\n     agent_workers → work_workers)\n\nThis portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the\nplan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — with #15\nlanding in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,\n`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue\nyet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the\nplan (lines 196–204) lists \"CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work\nworkers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)\" as separate work\nthat comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without\n(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence\nis reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and\nit strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new\nverbs.\n\n(d) \"Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to\n    new verbs\"\n\nThis depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,\n`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable\ncommands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and\n`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\\\"run\\\"` /\n`Use:.*\\\"try\\\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`\ninner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,\nscripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,\nscripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,\nlibrary/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call\nnon-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would\nspecifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced\nthis very plan (plan line 208).\n\n## What an unblocked attempt needs\n\nRoughly in dependency order:\n\n1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:\n   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,\n   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in\n   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive — see memory\n   feedback_no_local_replace).\n2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class\n   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the\n   plan).\n3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons\n   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then\n   ported in this bead.\n\nOnce those are in, the present bead becomes:\n\n  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`\n    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,\n    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,\n    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for\n    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).\n  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,\n    agent_route_status.go (the only \"delete with prejudice\" files\n    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/\n    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions\n    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go\n    rewrite).\n  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →\n    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);\n    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`\n    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.\n  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to\n    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` → `ddx run`,\n    `ddx agent execute-loop` → `ddx work`,\n    `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try`,\n    `ddx agent log/metrics/usage/executions/workers` → the new\n    `runs/tries/work workers` paths). Specific known references:\n    scripts/eval-skill.sh, scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh,\n    scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh, scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,\n    library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md,\n    library/personas/README.md, cli/cmd/init.go:757,\n    cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30–33, plus a wider grep across\n    docs/**/*.md and library/**/*.md.\n\n## What is done in this attempt\n\nNothing on disk — but the verification above (audit cross-reference,\nagent_cmd.go wiring inspection, FEAT-001 surface inspection,\nsearch for top-level `run`/`try` registrations) is captured here so\nthe next attempt can move directly into execution once bead #6 lands.\n\n## Recommended next steps\n\n- Park this bead behind bead #6 (upstream CONTRACT-003-A amendment)\n  and the P2 implementation beads that introduce top-level\n  `ddx run` / `ddx try` and the `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`\n  namespace skeletons.\n- Re-attempt with the same id once those prerequisites have merged.\n  At that point this bead is mechanical: a contained refactor of\n  agent_cmd.go plus a directed grep-and-edit sweep of the call sites\n  enumerated above.\nresult_rev=a2d7da5e8a5ef09edfde6bb416eb9fd0a018d818\nbase_rev=a2d7da5e8a5ef09edfde6bb416eb9fd0a018d818","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:47:01.771728126Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"already_satisfied"}],"execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-ebf3c09b","issue_type":"task","labels":["impl","plan-2026-04-29","cli","migration"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-829c29d9","status":"closed","title":"[artifact-run-arch] repo-wide verb migration + agent_*.go cleanup","updated_at":"2026-04-30T00:47:01.919986374Z"}

agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=15844 cost_usd=1.8110"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\" ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) \"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. The bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. ## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo) Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable): ### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID) Scope: - Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env). - Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works. - Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl. - Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess. - Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path. - Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green. Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC. ### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec Scope: - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct. - Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling. - Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal. Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green. Estimated: ~300–500 LOC. ### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude) Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires: - Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port. - Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead. - POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once. - Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status). - Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH. Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented. Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra. ### Dependencies - 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec) - 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape) ## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree. ## Bookkeeping A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.","created_at":"2026-05-03T06:56:53.03277574Z","kind":"no_changes_needs_investigation","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes_needs_investigation"},{"actor":"erik","body":"no_changes\nrationale: status: needs_investigation\nreason: Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs.\n\n## Why this attempt declined to commit\n\nThe bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1108:{"acceptance":"1. cli/cmd/try.go exists implementing 'ddx try \u003cbead-id\u003e' as a cobra subcommand. Help text describes operator-targeted execution + the relationship to 'ddx work'.\n\n2. 'ddx try unknown-bead-id' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead not found: unknown-bead-id'.\n\n3. 'ddx try \u003cclosed-bead-id\u003e' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead is not claimable (status=closed)'. Same shape for cancelled.\n\n4. 'ddx try \u003cbead-id-with-unmet-deps\u003e' exits non-zero with stderr 'bead has unmet dependencies: \u003clist\u003e'.\n\n5. 'ddx try \u003cready-bead-id\u003e' claims the bead, executes via the existing execute-bead-loop machinery (one iteration, one bead), writes evidence to .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/, and exits with the bead's terminal status code (0 = success/already_satisfied; 1 = preserved_for_review/no_changes; 2 = execution_failed).\n\n6. Flag plumbing: --harness, --model, --profile, --provider, --effort, --opaque-passthrough, --no-review, --review-harness, --review-model. Each flag, when set, plumbs identically to the corresponding 'ddx work' flag (same struct fields in the runtime config).\n\n7. WIRED-IN: the new 'ddx try' command is registered in cli/cmd/root.go (or wherever the cobra root command assembles subcommands). Verified by 'ddx try --help' output AND by 'git grep \"tryCmd\\|NewTryCommand\" cli/cmd/' showing the registration.\n\n8. Tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go:\n   - TestTry_BeadNotFound — invalid ID returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit\n   - TestTry_BeadClosed — closed bead returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit\n   - TestTry_BeadUnmetDeps — bead with unclosed dep returns the documented stderr + non-zero exit\n   - TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes — fixture bead + stub executor; assert bead is claimed, attempted, and outcome reported (use the existing test fixtures from agent_execute_loop_test.go as a template)\n   - TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough — set --harness=codex --model=gpt-5.4-mini and assert these reach the runtime config (capture via stub)\n\n9. Quality gates:\n   - cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...\n   - lefthook run pre-commit\n\n10. Conventional commit message ending [\u003cthis-bead-id\u003e]. Stage cli/cmd/try.go + cli/cmd/try_test.go + any cli/cmd/root.go registration line specifically; DO NOT use git add -A.\n\n11. Manual verification (operator runs after merge): 'ddx try ddx-50da9674' (closed fixture-repo bead) returns non-zero with the closed-bead message; 'ddx try \u003csome-ready-bead\u003e' actually claims and executes it.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T20:06:45Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"ff5e64cecc17f3094f3933986966b94a50dc4f59","created_at":"2026-05-03T20:06:37.62309311Z","description":"PROBLEM\nOperators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:\n- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead\n- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled\n- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log\n\nThis blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.\n\nCode today (relevant entry points):\n- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate\n- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:\n\n  ddx try \u003cbead-id\u003e [--harness=\u003cname\u003e] [--model=\u003cname\u003e] [--profile=\u003cname\u003e]\n\nBehavior:\n1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.\n2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.\n3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):\n   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.\n   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.\n   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.\n4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).\n5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)\n- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)\n- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)\n- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)\n- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)\n\nINTERSECTION\n- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.\n- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.\n- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep
