See docs/plan-svelte-migration.md Stage 2 bead #16.","events":[{"actor":"opus-reviewer","body":"Stage 2.2 (node/projects resolvers, commit 7e4ce6a) established the resolver pattern cleanly but left five decisions open that affect every subsequent resolver bead. Lock these in at Stage 2.3 (beads + beadsByProject) before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7.\n\n1. StateProvider interface split\n   Current: single StateProvider in graphql/resolver_node.go with 3 methods (GetNodeSnapshot/GetProjectSnapshots/GetProjectSnapshotByID). Stage 2.3 will add GetBeads/GetBeadByID/GetBeadsByProject — the interface will balloon to 50+ methods across all stages. Recommendation: split into per-resolver-group sub-interfaces (NodeState, BeadState, DocumentState, WorkerState, etc.) each defined next to its resolver file. The top-level Resolver.State field embeds a composite interface that combines them. File: introduce state_provider.go with the composite interface and move the existing StateProvider methods into a new NodeState interface.\n\n2. Stable-key cursors (Relay connections)\n   Current: resolver_node.go uses encodeCursor/decodeCursor that emit base64(\"cursor:\u003cslice-index\u003e\"). Slice-index cursors break under insert/delete between pages — fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads (mutated constantly). Stage 2.3 MUST use a stable-key cursor: base64 of (sort-key, id) or similar. Do not copy-paste the slice-index helpers from resolver_node.go; introduce stable-key helpers in a shared graphql/cursors.go and use them for beads/documents/commits/sessions.\n\n3. Snapshot struct duplication vs direct domain types\n   Current: graphql/resolver_node.go defines ProjectSnapshot that mirrors server.ProjectEntry field-for-field. Fine for 3 fields. Will become painful if every state type needs a shadow struct (Bead, Document, Worker, Session, ExecutionRun, Persona, Commit, ...). Decide now: either accept the snapshot pattern and live with duplication, OR let the graphql package depend on narrowly-factored state types directly. Pick ONE before Stage 2.3 so we do not end up with both patterns.\n\n4. r.State == nil nil-check\n   Current: resolver_node.go has if r.State == nil { return ...} guards in every method. Will copy-paste into 50 resolvers. Refactor: Resolver constructor rejects nil state, drop the runtime check. Trivial fix (one constructor), huge win in readability across 50 call sites.\n\n5. node(id) prefix dispatch\n   Current: node(id) switches on \"node-\"/\"proj-\" prefixes with an if-else chain. Stage 2.3 adds bead- prefix. Stage 2.4-2.7 add doc-, worker-, session-, exec-, persona- prefixes. The chain grows to 9 branches. Better: prefix-to-loader map (or a registry that each resolver file registers into from its init()). Lock this at 2.3 before the chain grows.\n\nNone of these are defects in 2.2. They are shape decisions that SHOULD be made at 2.3 to avoid technical debt accumulation across 2.4-2.7.","created_at":"2026-04-14T22:44:50.987280017Z","kind":"note","source":"Stage 2.2 opus review shape concerns — propagated to 2.3","summary":"Five load-bearing decisions from Stage 2.2 review that Stage 2.3 should lock in before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"stalled: no write activity after 30 consecutive read-only tool calls\nresult_rev=a009b4824d91969bd13e53e9afc9f0364e48268c\nbase_rev=a009b4824d91969bd13e53e9afc9f0364e48268c\nretry_after=2026-04-15T04:50:24Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T22:50:24.676293905Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"execution_failed"},{"actor":"vidar-coder-stall","body":"This is a distinct failure mode from the long-context hang seen on Stage 2.2. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1130:{"acceptance":"(1) A regression test at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go (or execute_bead_land_test.go) runs a simulated successful execute-bead attempt against a real temp git repo and asserts: (a) after the run returns, the working tree is clean (git status --porcelain is empty), and (b) at least one file under .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/ is present in git log --all --oneline for the attempt's result or successor commit. Test must fail on current HEAD (98e379b) and pass post-fix. (2) The same test uses bead tracker evidence to confirm the bead's closing_commit_sha still resolves after the merge (git cat-file -e). (3) cli/internal/server/workers_test.go TestWorkerManagerStartAndShow (or a sibling test) exercises the server-worker path and asserts the same clean-worktree guarantee. (4) Full test suite (cli: go test ./cmd ./internal/agent ./internal/server) passes. (5) Manual dogfooding: ddx agent execute-loop --once against a ready bead leaves git status --porcelain empty, and HEAD or HEAD^ contains at least one file under .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/. (6) No changes to AGENTS.md, FEAT-006, or skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md — the policy is already correct; this is implementation only.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ce25dda3c76977d7d66e0a39dc6e93b0f2b25183","created_at":"2026-04-15T15:34:35.535203989Z","description":"## Scope\n\nexecute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it\nmaterializes (`.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,\n`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run\nreturns. Successful bead commits reach main via the \"Merge bead\" path, but the\nevidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that\ngap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and\nreturns the working tree clean.\n\nConcrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill\nthat imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` directories\n(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.\nThat backfill must not need to happen again.\n\n## Files to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the\n  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and\n  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and\n  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:\n  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's\n      evidence files, single commit), OR\n  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt-id\u003e]` commit\n      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same\n      merge transaction.\n  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent\n  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must\n  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent\n  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded\n  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After\n  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,\n  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the\n  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/`),\n  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise (\"orchestrator left worktree\n  dirty: \u003cpath\u003e\").\n- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,\n  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and\n  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean\n  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping\n  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new\n  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.\n\n## Files NOT to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead\n  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /\n  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep\n  telling the agent to `git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e` only. The fix belongs in\n  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.\n- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,\n  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit\n  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.\n\n## Cross-Bead Context\n\n- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` (\"Evolve execute-bead into a tracked\n  execution-evidence workflow\", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this\n  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.\n- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md\n  \"Merge Policy\" section, FEAT-006 \"Execution Trail Preservation in\n  Downstream Merges\" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and\n  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md \"Branch Merge Policy\" section.\n- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and\n  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths\n  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick\n  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.\n- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc (\"anchor SessionLogDir to project root\") gives\n  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is\n  the analogous fix for execution evidence.\n\n## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)\n\n\u003e **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]\n\u003e Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git\n\u003e history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports\n\u003e read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`\n\u003e contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from\n\u003e git history alone.\n\u003e\n\u003e **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**\n\u003e - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker\n\u003e   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then\n\u003e   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target\n\u003e   branch HEAD after the merge.\n\u003e - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing\n\u003e   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e\n\u003e   \u003cclosing_commit_sha\u003e` still succeeds on main after the merge.\n\nThis bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the\nper-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely\npreserved across merges.\n\n## Verification Commands\n\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`\n  passes (new regression test, see AC below).\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes\n  (full suite; no regressions).\n- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run\n  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,\n  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show\n  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` in the HEAD commit\n  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"agent\",\"route_reason\":\"first-available\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"base_url\":\"http://vidar:1234/v1\"}","created_at":"2026-04-16T04:47:09.142456953Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"agent\",\"route_reason\":\"first-available\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"base_url\":\"http://bragi:1234/v1\"}","created_at":"2026-04-16T18:11:14.003181123Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"claude-opus-4-6\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-16T18:34:21.429059193Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=claude-opus-4-6"}],"id":"ddx-f7ac7826","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:exec","area:git"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"orchestrator: land execution evidence and leave worktree clean after agent run","updated_at":"2026-04-16T18:34:37.770774687Z"}

The flag is a no-op kept for compatibility, with the\n    behavior documented in the inline comment at bead.go:808-810.\n  - Implementation: archive.go:182-209 — union with active-wins precedence,\n    archive-missing tolerated as empty.\n  - Test: TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (archive_test.go:216-245)\n    exercises both the active-only List() and the merged ListWithArchive().\n\nAC4. ddx bead ready / blocked still operate only on the active collection.\n\n  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1224-1344 — Ready() / ReadyExecution() route\n    through readyFiltered() which calls s.ReadAll(), the active-collection\n    read. The archive partner is never consulted.\n  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1347-1469 — Blocked() / BlockedAll() also\n    call s.ReadAll(); no archive read-through.\n  - Test: TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly\n    (archive_test.go:185-214) seeds a closed-and-archived bead alongside an\n    open active bead, runs Archive() so the closed entry truly leaves the\n    active file, then asserts Ready() returns only the open bead and\n    Blocked() is empty. PASS.\n\nAC5. ddx work / agent execute-loop ignores archive when picking next work.\n\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:199 declares the worker's Store\n    interface as ReadyExecution()-only.\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1088 — w.Store.ReadyExecution()\n    is the queue source, which (per AC4) runs against the active collection\n    only.\n  - The same readyFiltered active-only ReadAll covers this path; no\n    additional plumbing reaches into the archive partner from the loop.\n\nAC6. New tests cover read-through for show, and active-only behavior for\nready/blocked.\n\n  - Read-through for show: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack\n    (archive_test.go:150).\n  - Active-only behavior for ready/blocked:\n    TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly (archive_test.go:185).\n  - Supporting coverage: TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads (line 36),\n    TestArchivePreservesReferencedClosedBeads (line 119),\n    TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (line 216),\n    TestArchiveOpportunisticTriggerOnClose (line 247).\n\nVerification\n------------\n\n  cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/...   → ok (4.933s)\n  cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/ -run TestBead  → ok (1.617s)\n\nWhy nothing was committed\n-------------------------\n\nThe bead description treats archive read-through as net-new work building on\nddx-2f453147. The earlier slice in this ADR-004 decomposition already shipped\nthe registry, the archive partner store, GetWithArchive / ListWithArchive,\nthe `bead show` and `bead list` wiring in cli/cmd/bead.go, the active-only\nbehavior of Ready/Blocked/ReadyExecution (which falls out of using ReadAll\non the active store), and the matching tests in archive_test.go. There is\nno remaining gap to fill for any of the six AC items, so committing\nadditional code or tests would be churn rather than progress.\n\nIf a future review disagrees and identifies a specific AC item that is in\nfact unmet, the natural next slice would be to add the missing piece (e.g.\na fully-isolated `--include-archive` flag distinct from the legacy `--all`\nno-op, or a dedicated cmd-level integration test for `bead show` against an\narchived ID). None of those gaps are visible in the current code.\nresult_rev=c69689ec723aa9854e9ea502e841caf4ad0013bf\nbase_rev=c69689ec723aa9854e9ea502e841caf4ad0013bf","created_at":"2026-05-02T04:50:57.800371782Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"already_satisfied"}],"execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-f7f09b6e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:beads","area:storage","kind:feature","adr:004"],"parent":"ddx-0c0565f3","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-d9718bbb","status":"closed","title":"Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)","updated_at":"2026-05-02T04:50:57.806347316Z"}

Workers running in a long-lived server context terminate on first empty-queue poll instead of staying alive for the next bead. This makes server-managed workers non-viable for persistent operation.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/work.go: default --poll-interval flag is 0 (or absent); drain mode exits on empty queue.\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go: GraphQL worker dispatch also defaults to poll_interval=0.\n- cli/internal/server/server.go: server-spawned workers use same default.\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: execute-loop base command also has poll_interval default at 0.\n- No_ready_work state is treated as a terminal exit (not a transient idle substate) in server-managed workers.\nddx-dc157075 proposed a 30s default tactically; this child (C10) subsumes it fully after C9 (ddx-9228a484) lands.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Change default --poll-interval to 30s in all four sites: cli/cmd/work.go, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go, cli/internal/server/server.go.\n- Preserve --once exit-on-empty behavior.\n- Preserve explicit --poll-interval=0 as legacy drain mode (backward compat).\n- Map server-managed workers' no_ready_work to running/idle substate (transient) instead of exited (terminal).\n- Close ddx-dc157075 as superseded after this lands.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- StopCondition enum (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484, a dep).\n- Frontend changes to worker display.","id":"ddx-f9acb86d","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"C10: ddx work stay-alive default + transient idle substate (subsumes ddx-dc157075 fully)","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:08:52.41207664Z"}

Find them and either:\n- File unification beads following the ExecuteLoopSpec pattern, OR\n- Document them as already-clean (and why).\n\nCOMMANDS TO AUDIT (per codex review — expanded)\n\n1. ddx agent run (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go ~395-414 cobra flags) PLUS its server endpoint /api/agent/run (cli/internal/server/server.go:2150-2188) AND its MCP equivalent (cli/internal/server/server.go:4537-4553). The server endpoint accepts only Harness/Prompt/Model/Effort while cobra has many more flags — same drop risk as ExecuteLoop, narrower fields.\n2. ddx work passthrough (alias for ddx agent execute-loop with implicit OpaquePassthrough — covered by ddx-29058e2a but verify the implicit behavior is documented in the spec)\n3. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) — covered by ddx-29058e2a explicitly, but audit any OTHER GraphQL adapters with similar patterns\n4. Server-side worker variants in cli/internal/server/workers.go — anything else that takes a Spec struct\n5. ddx bead create / update — flag set is small but check whether server endpoint mirrors\n6. ddx server commands (ddx server workers list/show/log) — read-only, less risk but verify\n7. ddx try — NOT shipped as a cobra command in this tree. Record as N/A; do not chase.\n8. ddx run — same: if not shipped, N/A.\n\nAUDIT METHOD (per codex — generated diff, not gut-feel)\n\nFor each command:\n1. Enumerate cobra flag list (programmatic: parse cobra command struct or grep for `cmd.Flags().*Var`).\n2. Enumerate corresponding server JSON request struct fields (programmatic: AST parse the request struct in handler).\n3. Enumerate the spec/persisted struct fields (programmatic).\n4. Enumerate the final consumer fields (programmatic).\n5. Diff each layer's field set; classify each field as: execution / control-plane / output-only / unsupported / dropped.\n6. Drop-risk score: count of fields that exist in cobra but NOT in subsequent layers.\n\nOUTPUT\n\nReport at .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/parallel-struct-audit.md:\n- One section per command audited\n- For each: layer count, list of fields per layer, classification table, drop-risk score, recommendation (unify / clean / N/A)\n- Summary table: command | layer count | drop-risk count | recommendation\n\nIf unification candidates found, file follow-up beads using the ExecuteLoopSpec refactor (ddx-29058e2a) as the template.\n\nDEP CHANGE (per codex)\n- DEP on ddx-29058e2a is RELAXED to \"may begin read-only command discovery + diff generation in parallel; only the implementation-pattern recommendations need ddx-29058e2a as reference.\" Update dep semantics so this can start now.","id":"ddx-fb290074","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:server","kind:audit","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"audit: parallel-struct smell — find other ddx commands with multi-layer flag wiring","updated_at":"2026-05-03T00:16:25.804859332Z"}

That SHA predates hx-f2b93e92 entirely and does not touch hx-f2b93e92 or the hx-951f30f9 tracker repair; it is the earlier hx-83ed9550 implementation commit that normalized short commit SHAs and repaired ddx-7e88c022. FEAT-019 says tracker-only backfills must not point closing_commit_sha at unrelated historical commits or later metadata-only closes, because replay/audit use the field as the governed work boundary. As recorded now, hx-f2b93e92 replays against unrelated bead-close code instead of representing a metadata-only repair finding.\nSuggested fix: Clear hx-f2b93e92.closing_commit_sha and leave the bead closed without replay provenance, or otherwise record only the actual governed implementation boundary if a non-metadata repair commit exists and is documented.","id":"hx-28525a7d","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: clear misleading closing provenance on hx-f2b93e92","updated_at":"2026-04-10T14:16:37.755368467Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1216:{"acceptance":"In a non-git working directory, a review bead that already has closing_commit_sha=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678 keeps that exact value after 'ddx bead close \u003cid\u003e'; metadata-only review closes in git repos still clear only positively identified '.ddx/beads.jsonl'-only backfill SHAs; and bead close acceptance tests cover both the non-git preserve path and the tracker-only clear path.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:58:41Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2318169","closing_commit_sha":"6d60638","created_at":"2026-04-10T10:57:00.4304316Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:150\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 93ac319 narrows metadata-only review-close cleanup by checking whether the existing `closing_commit_sha` points at a tracker-only backfill before deleting it, but `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` treats any `git show` failure as metadata-only (`return true`). Reproduced against the checked-out source by building `/tmp/ddx-review` from `cli/`, running it in a temp directory with `DDX_BEAD_DIR` and no git repo, creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678`, and then closing the bead. The bead closes successfully but the stored `closing_commit_sha` disappears. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for portable/manual review workflows: when git metadata is unavailable, a valid previously recorded implementation boundary is erased even though the command cannot prove it was a tracker-only backfill.\nSuggested fix: Make `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` fail open for provenance preservation when commit inspection is unavailable, or otherwise only clear an existing `closing_commit_sha` after positively proving the referenced commit is a `.ddx/beads.jsonl`-only backfill; add regression coverage for closing a review bead with a pre-existing full SHA in a non-git workspace.","id":"hx-2a146683","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve existing review provenance when git lookup is unavailable","updated_at":"2026-04-10T11:00:59.253241422Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1236:{"acceptance":"Given a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under `~/`, when `ddx install --local` runs inside a project, then both the global installed root and `.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` exist and project-relative plugin resource paths resolve","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T04:31:27Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"569068","closing_commit_sha":"8e1727512319acb8b916ebbebbccb7607924a9c7","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:21:30.705389801Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/install.go:244\nCategory: integration\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Registry installs explicitly create `.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` as a project-local symlink when the manifest root target is global (`~/...`) so project-relative plugin paths still resolve. The new manifest-driven local install path does not mirror that behavior: it creates the global symlink only. Repro: a local plugin whose manifest sets `install.root.target: ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin` produces `global_plugin_exists=yes`, `project_plugin_exists=no`, and still installs skills. Any code or docs that resolve plugin resources through `.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/...` now break in local developer installs.\nSuggested fix: When `installLocal` honors a global root target, also create the same project-local `.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` symlink that `InstallPackage` creates for registry installs.","id":"hx-3f3c0e74","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep project-local plugin path for local installs with global roots","updated_at":"2026-04-10T04:33:09.938605658Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1327:{"acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:50:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2270767","closing_commit_sha":"d51f18c93d88901d833c9cc82ab280611e7690d2","created_at":"2026-04-10T10:32:22.111979389Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:649\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.\nSuggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","id":"hx-939516c7","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:51:40.697890368Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1333:{"acceptance":"With git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, closing a bead whose landed implementation changes only tracked .ddx/* files still records closing_commit_sha for the landed close commit, and ddx agent replay can use that commit boundary instead of falling back to sessionless/manual mode.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T00:46:59Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3599950","closing_commit_sha":"9119c7cf3a95687f50ff3ed5d1f6353e502c74f6","created_at":"2026-04-10T00:29:53.073036931Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:80\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.\nSuggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.","id":"hx-98fec407","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:48:20.400206217Z"}

FEAT-019 says tracker-only backfills must omit closing_commit_sha rather than pointing replay/audit at an unrelated implementation boundary, so the last commit resolves the spec-id gap but leaves hx-cee7ffcd with misleading provenance.\nSuggested fix: Clear hx-cee7ffcd.closing_commit_sha through the supported ddx bead update path so the closed review-finding bead no longer advertises 6ddbee142e951ad7475b9d4f06d993b9ffa80b4d as its governed implementation commit.","id":"hx-ee91afab","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:docs","area:agent"],"parent":"ddx-650c94dc","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: clear misleading closing provenance on hx-cee7ffcd","updated_at":"2026-04-10T16:15:08.530801568Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1467:{"acceptance":"Given a plugin package has a valid package.yaml but a declared skill directory missing SKILL.md or required frontmatter, when `ddx install --local` or registry install runs, then the command exits non-zero, writes no installed.yaml entry, and surfaces the same structural validation error that doctor would report","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:33:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"234160","closing_commit_sha":"516fc8c8a7d25dbc47e3f8ba694bb2646b2c8d67","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:20:55.587100214Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/install.go:212\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: high\nDescription: The new install paths only validate `package.yaml`, then proceed to symlink/copy skills without reusing the structural checks that doctor performs. `installLocal` calls `LoadPackageManifest` and then links skills, while `InstallPackage` does the same for tarball installs. As a result, malformed plugins still install successfully. Repro: a local plugin with a valid package.yaml and a declared skill directory containing `bad-skill/` but no `SKILL.md` installs successfully instead of failing fast.\nSuggested fix: Share one structural validator between install and doctor so install rejects missing `SKILL.md`, invalid frontmatter, and related contract violations before writing install state.","id":"hx-f7e65bcc","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: fail install when plugin skill metadata is invalid","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:33:45.197279785Z"}

That bypasses the command's existing force guard and silently deletes any pre-existing project-local plugin checkout or files even when --force was not provided.\nEvidence: Reproduced by creating an existing worktree directory at .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin with a sentinel file, then running ddx install sample-plugin --local \u003cpath\u003e for a manifest rooted at ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin. The command returned success, sentinel_exists=false, and the directory was replaced by a symlink.\nSuggested fix: Apply the same collision policy used for the declared root target to the project-local compatibility path: only replace an existing entry when it is already a symlink or when --force is set, and add a regression test covering the non-force collision case.","id":"hx-fa0ffd40","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve existing project plugin dir unless --force is set","updated_at":"2026-04-10T09:09:01.361893198Z"}

package cmd

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "work",
		Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
		Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.

Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
within the requested power bounds.

Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
  drained     — no ready beads remain
  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts

work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
`,
		Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
  ddx work

  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
  ddx work --once

  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
  ddx work --poll-interval 30s

  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7

  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		RunE: f.runWork,
	}

	// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
	// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
	cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
	cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
	cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
	cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
	cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
	_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")
	cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
	cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
	cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
	// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
	cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
		"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
	// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
	// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
	cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
	cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")

	// Register "ddx work plan" as a subcommand of "ddx work".
	cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())

	return cmd
}

// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
// by this layer.
func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
}

  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-34d039b7",
    "title": "execute-bead: autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T04:43:00.09109294Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T06:03:25.675050303Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:enhancement",
      "area:agent",
      "area:workers",
      "root-cause:defense-in-depth"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-0a651925",
    "description": "## Residual risk after RC1–RC4 from ddx-0a651925\n\nEven with context propagation (RC1), wall-clock bounds (RC2), tightened breakers (RC3), and provider deadlines (RC4), a goroutine-level deadlock or panic-swallowed handler could still leave a worker alive past any reasonable bound. Today, `server.WorkerManager.Stop` (workers.go:648) only calls `handle.cancel()` — it has no escalation to OS-level signal and no autonomous trigger. ddx-b808df39 adds the *operator* tool; this bead adds the *autonomous* watchdog.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. WorkerManager runs a supervisor goroutine that, every minute, inspects each `workerHandle`.\n2. If `time.Since(record.StartedAt) \u003e WatchdogDeadline` (default e.g. 6h) AND `record.CurrentAttempt` has not transitioned in `StallDeadline` (default e.g. 1h), escalate:\n   - Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group (requires surfacing PID, aligned with ddx-b808df39).\n   - After 30s grace, send SIGKILL.\n   - Mark record.State = \"reaped\"; update claimed bead state so the claim is not leaked.\n3. Emit a `bead.reaped` event on the tracker with the full timeline so post-mortems are easy.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Per-bead runtime tuning; defaults are enough.\n- Replacing ddx-b808df39 — this is defense-in-depth, not duplication. ddx-b808df39 is operator-initiated; this bead is system-initiated.\n\n## Dependencies\n\n- ddx-b808df39 surfaces PID in `ddx agent workers --json` output; this bead reuses that plumbing.",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] WorkerManager starts a supervisor goroutine on first worker launch\n- [ ] Watchdog reaps workers past `WatchdogDeadline` with no phase transition in `StallDeadline`\n- [ ] SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation works on an intentionally-wedged test worker (unit test with a goroutine that ignores ctx)\n- [ ] Reaped worker's bead claim is released (tracker status returns to open or claim-expired)\n- [ ] `bead.reaped` event recorded on tracker with reason=\"watchdog\" and duration\n- [ ] Watchdog deadlines are configurable via ddx server config"
  },
  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-3e3913a0",
    "title": "execute-bead: audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T04:42:38.821147268Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T05:48:25.052955138Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:bug",
      "area:agent",
      "area:providers",
      "root-cause:rc4"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-0a651925",
    "description": "## Root cause RC4 from ddx-0a651925\n\nAfter RC1 is fixed (caller ctx propagates), provider HTTP calls still need per-request deadlines. A stalled TCP socket that delivers headers and then stops sending body bytes — particularly during streaming token reads — can hang a goroutine for days.\n\nGrepping the `DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14` module cache shows short timeouts only on discovery probes (`provider/openai/discovery.go:46,174`, `openai.go:123`). The main `Run` call path inside agent providers appears to inherit whatever ctx DDx passes — which today is `context.Background()` (see RC1).\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Audit `openai`, `anthropic`, `virtual` providers in the agent library for per-request deadline usage.\n2. Install a per-request deadline (default ~10-15 min) on each provider call; separate from the wall-clock bound in RC2.\n3. For streaming bodies, enforce an **idle-read limit** on the response body so a mid-stream stall cannot hold a goroutine indefinitely (e.g. `http.Transport.ResponseHeaderTimeout` + periodic `SetReadDeadline` on the underlying conn).\n4. Surface provider-timeout failures as a distinct status in `ExecuteBeadResult.Detail`.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Rewriting providers; minimally invasive change only.\n- Changing DDx-side timeout defaults (RC2 covers outer bound).\n\n## Prerequisite\n\nSibling RC1 bead must be resolved first, otherwise adding deadlines inside providers is useless while DDx still feeds them `context.Background()`.",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] Audit report (checked-in .md or bead-comment) lists every `http.Client.Do`-equivalent call in agent providers with its current deadline semantics\n- [ ] Each main provider call path installs a per-request deadline (default 15m or catalog-configurable)\n- [ ] Streaming body reads terminate within a bounded idle period (ReadDeadline or equivalent)\n- [ ] Integration test: fake HTTP server accepts the connection, sends headers, then stops responding. Provider call returns within deadline with a distinct error"
  },
  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1",
    "title": "execute-bead: propagate caller context through Runner.Run and RunAgent",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T04:41:24.464808955Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T05:15:25.287344649Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:bug",
      "area:agent",
      "area:workers",
      "root-cause:rc1"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-0a651925",
    "description": "## Root cause RC1 from ddx-0a651925\n\n`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (Runner.Run) and `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (Runner.RunAgent) both call `context.WithCancel(context.Background())`, discarding any context from the caller. `RunOptions` exposes no ctx field. `agent.ExecuteBead` likewise takes no ctx, so `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s ctx never reaches the agent call.\n\nAs a result, `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (server/workers.go:648) cancels only the worker goroutine; the embedded `agentlib.Run` call (and any subprocess it launched) keeps running until SIGKILL. This is the structural defect behind 33–142h hung workers.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Add `Context context.Context` to `RunOptions` (or change signatures to `Run(ctx, opts)`). Default to `context.Background()` when nil.\n2. Derive the internal ctx from the caller's rather than `context.Background()`.\n3. Thread ctx through `agent.ExecuteBead` → `runner.Run` → `agentlib.Run` → provider HTTP.\n4. Update `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s `singleTierAttempt` closure to pass `ctx` into `agent.ExecuteBead`.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- New wall-clock timeout logic (covered by sibling RC2 bead)\n- Operator-facing stop command (covered by ddx-b808df39)\n",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] `RunOptions` accepts a caller ctx, or `Runner.Run`/`Runner.RunAgent` accept ctx explicitly\n- [ ] The internal cancel is derived from the caller ctx (no `context.Background()` seeds inside Run/RunAgent)\n- [ ] `agent.ExecuteBead` takes ctx and passes it down\n- [ ] `server.WorkerManager.Stop(id)` cancels the running agent provider call within 2s in a unit test (fake provider blocks on ctx)\n- [ ] Regression test: runner with a canceled ctx returns promptly (\u003c500ms) regardless of mocked provider latency"
  },
  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b0562380",
    "title": "execute-bead: bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "ddx",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T04:41:53.916108666Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-19T22:56:41.167757535Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:enhancement",
      "area:agent",
      "root-cause:rc3"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-0a651925",
    "description": "## Root cause RC3 from ddx-0a651925\n\n`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures` (50 by default). Observed on bundle `20260416T230316-efbf91c5`: the breaker took **2h 8m** to fire (7,720,908 ms). If each iteration takes ~3 min to emit a no-op compaction event, 50 events = ~2.5h. The counter is useful but fires far too late to be a hang-guard.\n\n## Scope\n\nEither:\n1. **Time-bounded** — fire after N no-op events **within M minutes** (e.g. 10 within 15m), OR\n2. **Threshold lowered** — drop default from 50 to ~10 (only for agent harness where one iteration is already expensive), OR\n3. **Wall-clock guard layered on top** — independent breaker fires after e.g. 20 min of consecutive no-op compaction events regardless of count.\n\nRecommend option 1 or 3: time-based thresholds are more robust to provider-speed variance.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Changing the compaction algorithm itself.\n- Extending breakers outside the compaction path.",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] Compaction-stuck detection fires within 15 minutes when no-op compaction events are the only activity, regardless of event rate\n- [ ] No regression on the existing behaviour for healthy agents (normal runs do not trip)\n- [ ] Unit test: fake agentlib emits one no-op compaction event every 3 seconds → breaker fires within 15 min\n- [ ] Result.json `detail` clearly reports the time-based breaker when it fires"
  },
  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b203c889",
    "title": "execute-bead: add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T04:41:40.15942901Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T05:30:49.793036993Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:bug",
      "area:agent",
      "area:workers",
      "root-cause:rc2"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-0a651925",
    "description": "## Root cause RC2 from ddx-0a651925\n\nThe `DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000` (2h) in `cli/internal/agent/types.go:363` is advertised as a hard cap but implemented as an *idle timeout*:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/executor.go:154-178` resets `time.NewTimer(idleTimeout)` on every stdout/stderr byte.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:264-285` resets on every non-compaction agentlib event.\n\nA provider that emits any heartbeat, retry line, or periodic event defeats the timer indefinitely — enabling the 33–142h hangs observed on agent projects.\n\nOnly the claude streaming path (`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:373-384`) uses a true wall-clock deadline via `time.After(timeout)`. That path is why bundle `20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba` fired at exactly 2h 0m 0.098s — and nothing else does.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Add an absolute wall-clock deadline in addition to (not replacing) the idle timeout.\n2. Default the wall-clock bound to something operators can trust — e.g. 3x `DefaultTimeoutMS` or a new `DefaultWallClockMS`.\n3. Apply in: `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` (subprocess path) and `Runner.RunAgent` (embedded agent path).\n4. Reflect the wall-clock error in result.json as a distinct `error: \"wall-clock deadline exceeded after Xh\"` so it is not confused with idle timeout.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Lowering or removing the existing idle timeout.\n- Per-provider tuning (can be follow-up).",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] New default wall-clock deadline (e.g. 3h) applied to both `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` and `Runner.RunAgent`\n- [ ] Wall-clock timeout fires even when stdout/stderr/agent events are flowing (unit test: fake provider emits 1 event/sec for \u003e deadline, expect cancel at deadline ± 1s)\n- [ ] Idle timeout behaviour preserved (unit test: no activity for idle timeout → fire)\n- [ ] Result.json distinguishes `timeout: wall-clock` from `timeout: idle`\n- [ ] Documented in types.go (DefaultWallClockMS constant with comment)"
  },

  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-22cadb21",
    "title": "routing: structured-output (JSON-mode) gating — likely incidental to tool-calling",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 4,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T06:50:54.195936927Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T22:43:47.191941677Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:implementation",
      "area:agent",
      "area:routing"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-4817edfd",
    "description": "## Lowest-priority sibling of ddx-4817edfd.\n\nSome provider/model combos don't implement JSON-mode / response_format=json_object / tool-use-required. If a bead prompt depends on structured output, today routing's blindness means the failure surfaces at dispatch.\n\nPer the parent bead's analysis, this is probably covered incidentally by the tool-calling gating sub-bead — most reasoning-tool-capable models also support structured output. This bead exists to either (a) explicitly add a SupportsStructuredOutput capability, or (b) close as 'covered by tool-calling gating' once that bead lands and we verify the assumption.\n\n## Decision gate (do this first)\n\nBefore writing code, verify the 'covered by tool-calling' hypothesis:\n- Survey the providers we currently route to (anthropic, openai, openrouter, omlx, lmstudio, ollama) and check whether each one's tool-calling support implies JSON-mode support.\n- If yes everywhere we care about: close this bead with a note in the tool-calling bead's tests / docs that asserts the implication.\n- If no (any counter-example): proceed with scope below.\n\n## Scope (only if decision gate fails)\n\n- Add SupportsStructuredOutput tri-state to ModelEntry / Surface and to capabilities.go's curated table.\n- RouteRequest.RequiresStructuredOutput bool; execute-bead sets it when the prompt requires JSON mode.\n- evaluateCandidate rejects RequiresStructuredOutput=true on SupportsStructuredOutput=no. Unknown passes with -v warning.\n\n## Files (only if decision gate fails)\n\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go\n- cli/internal/agent/routing.go\n- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go\n- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go",
    "acceptance": "1. EITHER: closed as 'covered by tool-calling gating' with a written justification (e.g., test or comment in the tool-calling bead's deliverables that asserts SupportsTools =\u003e SupportsStructuredOutput for all currently routed providers).\n2. OR: SupportsStructuredOutput tri-state added to ModelEntry/Surface + capabilities table, with rejection logic in evaluateCandidate, RouteRequest.RequiresStructuredOutput field, and unit tests parallel to the tool-calling bead's tests.\n3. go test ./cli/... is green (only applies to outcome 2).",
    "notes": "Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing"
  },
  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-854cb8a7",
    "title": "routing: tool-calling support gating with per-flavor capability metadata",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T06:50:30.504525579Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T22:43:47.285105492Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:implementation",
      "area:agent",
      "area:routing",
      "area:catalog"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-4817edfd",
    "description": "## Reject routes that can't actually run tool-calling work (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).\n\nSome omlx flavors and older local OpenAI-compat servers do not implement /v1/chat/completions with tools. Today routing fuzzy-matches them and dispatch fails with 'tools not supported' — or worse, silently returns a no-tool response that the execute-bead loop cannot consume.\n\n## Scope\n\n- Per-provider / per-flavor capability metadata: SupportsTools bool. Two layers:\n  1. Hand-curated table for known harnesses (claude, codex, opus, etc.) and provider flavors (openai, anthropic, openrouter, omlx, lmstudio, ollama).\n  2. Discovery-driven enrichment when /v1/models/status (or equivalent) advertises tool support per model.\n- RouteRequest gains RequiresTools bool (default false to preserve current behavior; execute-bead sets true).\n- evaluateCandidate rejects candidates where RequiresTools=true and the resolved (provider, flavor, model) combo is known NOT to support tools. Unknown = pass with -v warning, same pattern as context-window bead.\n- Capability metadata location: cli/internal/agent/catalog.go on ModelEntry/Surface, plus a curated table in cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (or co-located).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Context-window, structured-output, effort, permissions gating (sibling beads).\n- Probing servers actively at routing time (uses cached discovery + table).\n\n## Files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go (RouteRequest.RequiresTools, capability fields on CandidatePlan)\n- cli/internal/agent/routing.go (rejection in rejectIfNotViable / evaluateCandidate)\n- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go (SupportsTools on ModelEntry / Surface)\n- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (NEW — curated table)",
    "acceptance": "1. ModelEntry / Surface in catalog.go gain SupportsTools (tri-state: yes / no / unknown).\n2. capabilities.go (new file) ships a curated default table for known harness+flavor pairs.\n3. Discovery code populates SupportsTools from /v1/models/status when the field is present in the response.\n4. RouteRequest.RequiresTools added; execute-bead sets it true for bead execution.\n5. evaluateCandidate rejects RequiresTools=true candidates known to be SupportsTools=no, with a debug reason.\n6. Unknown SupportsTools does not reject; logs at -v.\n7. Unit tests cover: known-no rejection, known-yes pass, unknown pass with warning, RequiresTools=false disables the check.\n8. go test ./cli/... is green.",
    "notes": "Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing"
  },
  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-bb230ad5",
    "title": "routing: effort/permissions gating per provider+model (not just per harness)",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 3,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T06:50:41.86041297Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T22:43:47.3806568Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:implementation",
      "area:agent",
      "area:routing"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-4817edfd",
    "description": "## Provider-specific honoring of --effort and --permissions (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).\n\nToday, Harness.EffortFlag is checked at routing.go:rejectIfNotViable, so harnesses without effort support are rejected when --effort is set. But provider-level gating is missing: e.g., openrouter doesn't honor --effort on non-reasoning models, and --permissions unrestricted may not apply to every harness+provider combination. Routing should reject or downgrade rather than dispatch and silently no-op the flag.\n\n## Scope\n\n- Add effort/permissions capability fields to the curated capability table (same file added by the tool-calling sub-bead, or co-locate here if that one ships first).\n  - SupportsEffort tri-state per (provider, model).\n  - SupportsPermission(permission_class) — at minimum 'unrestricted'.\n- evaluateCandidate / rejectIfNotViable: when RouteRequest names an --effort or --permissions value not honored by the resolved (provider, model) combo, reject with a clear reason. Unknown = pass with -v warning.\n- Document expected behavior for each known provider in capabilities.go comments (openrouter non-reasoning models = no effort, etc.).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Context-window, tool-calling, structured-output gating (sibling beads).\n- Building a 'downgrade' path (e.g., silently strip --effort and continue). Rejection is sufficient for v1; downgrade is a follow-up.\n\n## Files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/routing.go\n- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (shared with tool-calling bead — or new file if order differs)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go",
    "acceptance": "1. Capability table tracks SupportsEffort and SupportsPermission per (provider, model_pattern).\n2. evaluateCandidate rejects candidates that don't honor a RouteRequest's --effort or --permissions, with a debug reason that names which capability and which provider/model.\n3. Unknown capability does NOT reject; logs at -v.\n4. Unit tests cover: known-no-effort rejection, known-yes pass, unknown pass with warning, no-effort-requested disables the check (parallel structure for permissions).\n5. go test ./cli/... is green.",
    "notes": "Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing"
  },
  {

      "kind:implementation",
      "area:agent",
      "area:routing"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-4817edfd",
    "description": "## Smallest useful slice of capability gating (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).\n\nRouting currently does not check whether the resolved model's context window can hold the bead prompt. A 32k-context fuzzy match against an 80k-token bead prompt routes 'successfully' and fails mid-dispatch with a provider 400 or truncated output.\n\n## Scope\n\n- Add optional EstimatedPromptTokens (uint32) to RouteRequest (cli/internal/agent/types.go).\n- In evaluateCandidate (cli/internal/agent/routing.go), when EstimatedPromptTokens is set and \u003e 0, reject (or penalize) candidates whose concrete_model context window is below it.\n- Source of context window per candidate: v0.3.11's LookupModelLimits first, then catalog ModelEntry.ContextWindow (cli/internal/agent/catalog.go) as fallback. If neither is known, treat as 'unknown' — do NOT reject (avoid false negatives), but log at -v.\n- Caller side: pass EstimatedPromptTokens from execute-bead when the bead prompt is assembled (best-effort — can use char/4 heuristic if a tokenizer is unavailable).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Tool-calling, structured-output, effort, permissions gating (separate sibling beads).\n- Adding a tokenizer dependency. char/4 estimate is fine for v1.\n\n## Files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go\n- cli/internal/agent/routing.go\n- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (or wherever the bead prompt is assembled)",
    "acceptance": "1. RouteRequest has EstimatedPromptTokens uint32 field, zero value means 'unspecified'.\n2. evaluateCandidate rejects candidates whose known context window \u003c EstimatedPromptTokens; emits a routing-debug reason that names the model and its window.\n3. Unknown context window does NOT cause rejection; logs a warning at -v.\n4. execute-bead populates EstimatedPromptTokens with at least a char/4 estimate of the assembled prompt.\n5. Unit test in routing_test.go covers: (a) below-window rejection, (b) above-window pass, (c) unknown-window pass with warning, (d) EstimatedPromptTokens=0 disables the check.\n6. go test ./cli/... is green.",
    "notes": "Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing"
  },

  {
    "id": ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T225413-91120fa0-5be5f460",
    "title": "ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker exec's ddx work subprocess (parent-side plumbing)",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-03T22:59:39.685160971Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-03T23:00:18.503933642Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:server",
      "kind:refactor",
      "adr:022"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-eccc6efb",
    "description": "PROBLEM: cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker runs the bead-execution loop in-process, knitting together LandCoordinator, DefaultBeadReviewer, CostCapTracker, RoutePreflight, ExecuteBeadWorker, ProgressCh→drainProgress, WakeCh, BeadWorkerFactory injection, and watchdog phase-stall tracking — all of which contradict ADR-022's autonomous-default model where workers are subprocesses identical to operator-launched 'ddx work'.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-940 (runWorker) builds an in-process executor closure (singleTierAttempt at lines 679-747), wraps it with a CostCap tracker (lines 753-797), wires a DefaultBeadReviewer (lines 801-809), and calls worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{...}). All of this duplicates work that 'ddx work' (cli/cmd/work.go → cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:runAgentExecuteLoopImpl) already does, just inside the parent server process.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Replace the in-process worker.Run(...) call with exec.CommandContext(ctx, dddxBin, 'work', \u003cflags\u003e) where dddxBin = os.Executable() (override-able via DDX_EXECUTABLE env for tests). Project root passed via --project flag or DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env. Provider/harness/model/profile/effort/poll-interval/once/no-review/review-harness/review-model/label-filter/model-ref translated to existing ddx work flags. Capture child PID into WorkerRecord.PID (already a field at workers.go:147). Stop() and the watchdog already signal via PID (workers.go:1070, 1279) — verify the cross-process path works; SIGTERM with kill grace then SIGKILL.\n\nProgress event ingestion changes: child process emits via workerprobe to /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event (this endpoint exists, server.go:884). The parent's WorkerManager subscribes to those events (in-memory derived view from the workers ingestion handler) and forwards into the per-subprocess workerHandle.progressCh so existing SSE subscribers and drainProgress logic continue to work. Mapping: parent records the WorkerRecord.id at exec time; child registers with /api/workers/register and gets a probe-issued worker_id; parent correlates by ProjectRoot + ExecutorPID + StartedAt window.\n\nDrop in-process LandCoordinator/Reviewer/CostCap/RoutePreflight wiring inside runWorker (they now run inside the child). LandCoordinators registry stays for the existing inline 'ddx work' path (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1714).\n\nWakeCh: replace with SIGUSR1 to the child (or document as no-op for the v1 of this refactor; WakeProject still walks workers but only sends to live in-process handles — kept null-safe for subprocess workers).\n\nBeadWorkerFactory: removed. Tests that previously injected a fake executor migrate to either (a) reentry-stub mode where the test binary acts as 'ddx work' via go test re-exec pattern, or (b) deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now live in cli/cmd/* tests).\n\nNON-SCOPE: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker rewrite (covered by 7b). Deletion of ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct (covered by 7b). End-to-end real-claude integration (covered by 7c). UI/GraphQL surface changes (already covered by ADR-022 step 5).",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker exec's the resolved ddx binary (os.Executable() with DDX_EXECUTABLE override) with subcommand 'work' and a flag-vector derived from ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. No in-process call to worker.Run remains in this code path.\n2. WorkerRecord.PID is populated with the subprocess PID before the exec returns to StartExecuteLoop; signal-based Stop and watchdog reaping continue to work against the child PID (TestStopExecuteLoopWorker_KillsSubprocess covers SIGTERM→SIGKILL grace).\n3. Progress events from the child reach the parent's drainProgress goroutine via the existing /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event ingestion path (server.go:884). At least one new test TestRunWorker_ProgressBridge_FromChild asserts that an event POSTed by the child appears on a subscribed SSE channel.\n4. BeadWorkerFactory is deleted from WorkerManager. workers_test.go/workers_e2e_test.go/workers_stop_propagation_test.go/workers_route_preflight_test.go/workers_config_test.go/graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go updated: tests that depended on injecting an in-process executor are rewritten using the test-reentry pattern (ddx-work-stub) or deleted with rationale comment if the semantics moved into cli/cmd/* tests.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes."
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-0097af14",
    "title": "execute-loop: land_conflict should attempt resolution before discarding preserved iteration",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-29T18:16:41.722078707Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-30T00:54:11.857367761Z",
    "labels": [
      "execute-loop",
      "beads",
      "quality-of-life",
      "area:agent"
    ],
    "description": "When ddx agent execute-bead lands a commit locally but merging into the base branch produces conflicts, the loop today preserves the result at refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003cts\u003e-\u003cbase\u003e and unclaims the bead with status: land_conflict. The bead reopens; subsequent attempts re-execute from scratch — burning more agent time and dollars even though the original work is intact in the preserved ref.\n\nOBSERVED 2026-04-29 on ddx-52469547 (FEAT-015 bead 3, 'runtime fallback removal + audit-side migration'):\n- Sonnet ran for ~3h, produced commit 12fbf6dd1b98 covering all 7 AC items.\n- The base branch advanced significantly during the run (V1, V2, V3 vision beads + dogfood + concurrent work).\n- The run hit the agent's 3h hard timeout right at the test phase.\n- execute-loop preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-52469547/20260429T175646Z-aa7b31785a44 with status execution_failed (timeout, but functionally identical to land_conflict for the merge story).\n- Operator attempted git merge --no-ff 12fbf6dd, hit 7 file-level conflicts (mechanical drift from parallel beads), aborted, and only avoided wasting the work because a parallel attempt (263b5eff) had landed the exact same scope cleanly. That parallel landing is a coincidence, not a guarantee.\n\nThe general case: a preserved iteration represents real money spent and real work done. land_conflict / execution_failed-with-result that bails to 'reopen and re-run' throws that work away. The loop should at least attempt to reuse the preserved result.\n\nREQUESTED BEHAVIOR\n\n1. On land_conflict (merge produces conflicts when applying the iteration onto current tip), execute-loop should:\n   a. Try a 3-way merge of the preserved iteration ref onto current main using merge.conflictStyle=diff3 and ort strategy with -X ours/-X theirs heuristics. Only if the merge resolves cleanly under those heuristics, commit it as a non-fast-forward merge with a message like 'Merge preserved iteration ddx-\u003cid\u003e after base drift'.\n   b. If 3-way auto-resolution fails, dispatch a SMALL focused agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files, the preserved iteration's commit message, and the bead AC. Its sole job: resolve the conflicts, verify build/tests, commit. Do NOT re-do the bead from scratch.\n   c. Only if (a) and (b) both fail, fall back to today's behavior (preserve, unclaim, set short cooldown, mark for human review with a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed — perhaps kind:land-conflict-unresolvable).\n\n2. Cooldown for land_conflict-unresolvable should be short (5-30 min), not the 24h cap, since the conflict will resolve faster as soon as a human or another bead lands a clarifying change.\n\n3. Same logic should apply to execution_failed-with-preserved-result (timeouts mid-merge): if a commit was produced before the failure, attempt to merge it before discarding the run.\n\n4. Reuse the auto-recover plumbing landed for push race in ddx-a458af7c (landPushAutoRecover, capLoopCooldown). Specifically the conflict-vs-clean-recovery branching should mirror landPushAutoRecover's structure.\n\n5. Regression tests:\n   - Fake git that produces a mechanical conflict resolvable via -X ours: assert auto-resolution succeeds and bead lands.\n   - Fake git that produces an irreconcilable structural conflict: assert escalation to focused-resolve agent (or, at minimum, that today's behavior is taken with the new structured outcome).\n   - Preserved-iteration-on-timeout case: assert the preserved commit IS attempted as a merge before falling back.\n\nACCEPTANCE OF FAILURE: if the focused-resolve agent itself fails or escalates to BLOCKING, that's a structured outcome (kind:land-conflict-needs-human) — the bead is parked with the conflict context and preserved ref clearly recorded.\n\nRelated: ddx-a458af7c (push race auto-recover, this session's predecessor work).",
    "acceptance": "1. execute-loop attempts 3-way auto-resolution of preserved iteration onto current tip before discarding (ort strategy + sane heuristics). 2. On 3-way failure, execute-loop dispatches a focused conflict-resolve agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files + AC + iteration commit message; on success, commits the merge. 3. Only after (1) and (2) fail does the loop park the bead. Park outcome is kind:land-conflict-unresolvable (or kind:land-conflict-needs-human if the focused-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING). 4. Cooldown for land-conflict-unresolvable is 5-30 min, capped under the 24h cap from ddx-a458af7c. 5. Same code path applies to execution_failed when the agent produced a commit before failing (timeout-with-preserved-commit case). 6. Regression tests: (a) mechanical conflict auto-resolves, (b) structural conflict escalates to focused-resolve, (c) timeout-with-preserved-commit attempts the merge. 7. CHANGELOG entry. 8. Update FEAT-006 (or wherever the execute-loop outcome contract is documented) to list the new structured outcomes.",
    "notes": "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff at rev 1550c5382a is a bare merge commit with no code changes; none of the 8 AC items (3-way auto-resolve, focused-resolve agent dispatch, structured outcomes, cooldown cap, timeout-with-preserved-commit path, regression tests, CHANGELOG, FEAT-006 update) can be evaluated as implemented."
  },
