    "id": "ddx-12cae4dd",
    "title": "Web UI documents view includes .claude/worktrees noise and serves absolute paths",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-22T20:06:21.727193482Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-24T02:56:56.033759006Z",
    "labels": [
      "feat-008",
      "feat-007",
      "ui",
      "docgraph"
    ],
    "description": "## Observed\n\nBrowsing the web UI to a document URL renders `Document not found` instead of content. The URL contains a worktree path with a leading double slash, e.g.:\n\n  /nodes/\u003cnode\u003e/projects/\u003cproj\u003e/documents//Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0673989/docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md\n\nTwo distinct defects:\n\n1. **`.claude/worktrees/` pollution** — the documents list surfaces markdown files from AI agent worktrees checked out under `.claude/worktrees/`. These are throwaway copies of the repo; they are noise and frequently shadow / duplicate the real `docs/` tree.\n2. **Absolute document paths** — document IDs/paths are absolute (`/Users/erik/...`), producing malformed URLs (leading double slash) and failing the reader resolver.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:247` (`findMarkdownFiles`) walks `workingDir` and only skips `.git` and `.ddx`. It traverses `.claude/` (including `.claude/worktrees/\u003cagent\u003e/…`) and records every `.md` it finds.\n\n`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:128` stores `doc.Path = filepath.Clean(filePath)`. Because `filepath.Walk` hands back paths rooted at the absolute `workingDir`, `doc.Path` ends up absolute. The GraphQL document resolver surfaces this absolute path as the document's id/path, which the frontend then interpolates into a URL.\n\n## Scope of fix\n\n- Exclude `.claude/` (and any `worktrees/` subtree) from doc discovery. `.claude` is a tool-managed directory analogous to `.git`/`.ddx`; it is not a source of canonical project documents.\n- Normalize `doc.Path` to be relative to `workingDir` so routes stay clean and portable.\n- No change required on the frontend beyond existing URL handling once paths are relative.",
    "acceptance": "**User story:** As a developer browsing the DDx web UI, I want the documents list to show only canonical project documents under the configured roots, so I can find `docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md` without seeing duplicates from agent worktrees or broken absolute-path links.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. `findMarkdownFiles` skips `.claude` (in addition to `.git` and `.ddx`) and does not descend into any `worktrees/` directory regardless of parent.\n2. `doc.Path` stored in the graph is relative to `workingDir`; no document path begins with `/`.\n3. GraphQL `documents` query returns zero entries whose path contains `.claude/worktrees/` when the repo has active worktrees under `.claude/worktrees/`.\n4. GraphQL `document(id:)` resolver handles both the previously-absolute form and the new relative form for a grace period (read-side tolerance) OR all callers are updated atomically.\n5. Web UI document route `/documents/:path` renders the correct document content for `docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md` (no double slash, no 404).\n6. Unit test in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` creates a fixture with `.claude/worktrees/agent-x/docs/foo.md` and `docs/foo.md`, and asserts only the latter appears in the graph with a relative path.\n7. Playwright e2e test in `cli/internal/server/frontend/` navigates to the documents list, asserts no listed document has a path containing `.claude/` or starting with `/`, clicks a real `docs/` document, and verifies the content renders.",
    "notes": "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**All AC items**: The merged diff consists solely of `.ddx/executions/20260423T014514-bc0872e9/result.json`, which is execution bookkeeping. Zero functional changes were landed — no modifications to `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go`, no GraphQL resolver updates, no frontend changes, no tests. The execution result claims `\"outcome\": \"task_succeeded\"` but the merge commit contains none of the required implementation. The work branch may have been incorrectly merged or the agent's changes were lost during the merge process. Investigate whether the implementation commits from `5ae2fb8c` were actually included in the merge at `4671d2db`.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go` / `findMarkdownFiles` is not changed, so `.claude` and arbitrary `worktrees/` directories are still not proven excluded.\n`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go` document path construction is not changed, so `doc.Path` is still not proven relative to `workingDir`.\nThe GraphQL documents and `document(id:)` resolver code is not changed, so `.claude/worktrees/` filtering and absolute-ID compatibility are not implemented in this diff.\n`cli/internal/server/frontend/` is not changed, and no Playwright test is added for the documents route.\n`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` is not changed, so the required fixture and assertions for `.claude/worktrees/agent-x/docs/foo.md` versus `docs/foo.md` are missing."
  },

    "status": "closed",

    "description": "\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved on 2026-04-11 while launching server-managed execute-loop workers against the restarted 8094 server. Worker records remained state=running with current_bead=null even after distinct execution bundles and manifests had already been created for claimed beads.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nWorker API projection lags behind real execution start. When a server-managed execute-loop worker has already claimed a bead and created an execution bundle, /api/agent/workers and /api/agent/workers/:id should expose that current bead immediately instead of waiting for end-of-run summary parsing.\n\n## Scope\n- Project current_bead as soon as the worker claims or launches a bead\n- Keep worker detail/list views aligned with execution bundle state while a run is active\n- Add regression coverage for running workers whose current bead is known before process exit\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\nServer-managed execute-loop workers expose current_bead promptly while running, not only after exit or summary parsing, and tests cover the live-running projection path."

  {
    "id": "ddx-13ff86c6",
    "title": "Generalize test-engineer persona — strip DDx-internal path references",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-17T22:39:32.555980009Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:implementation",
      "area:personas"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-94c28517",
    "description": "The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).\n\nRename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.\n\n**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**\n- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.\n- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.\n- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.\n- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.\n- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.\n- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.\n- Test names describe behavior (\"creates invoice when all line items valid\"), not positions (\"test_create_invoice_3\").\n- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.\n- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is \"fast\", \"scales\", or \"improved performance\" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. \"It feels fast\" and \"benchmarks look good\" are not performance claims.\n\n**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**\n- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (\"\u003e= 80% overall\", \"100% on critical paths\"). Replace with: \"measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target\".\n- Specific timing budgets as universal rules (\"unit \u003c 10ms\", \"integration \u003c 100ms\"). Replace with: \"tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern\".\n- Specific pyramid percentages (\"60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e\") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.\n- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD \"worst practice\".\n- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).\n- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.\n- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.\n- Retry loops around flaky tests as \"improvement\" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.\n- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say \"coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs\".\n\n**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**\n- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic \"a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary\")\n- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base\n- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md\n- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent\n\nGround in external canon:\n- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example\n- Martin Fowler - \"Mocks Arent Stubs\" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)\n- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)\n- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: test-engineer\nroles: [test-engineer, quality-analyst]\ndescription: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement\ntags: [testing, quality, performance]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/test-engineer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: deletion of test-engineer-tdd.md (deprecation bead); helix-test-engineer plugin persona (HELIX concern)",
    "acceptance": "File library/personas/test-engineer.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter. Body covers the core stance: favor real e2e tests (no mocks at e2e layer); stubs over mocks at unit layer; contract tests between stub and real backend; zero mocks in integration tests; behavior over implementation; deterministic factories; behavior-naming; testing pyramid as shape not ratios; performance measurements require baseline + explicit boundary + reproducible harness. grep -E \"cli/internal/|docs/helix/|orchTestGitOps|fakeExecuteBeadGit|gateTestGitOps|mockExecutor|script\\.go|integration_helper_test\" library/personas/test-engineer.md returns no matches. No hard numeric coverage threshold presented as a universal rule. No \"TDD is the only valid approach\" framing. No retry-loop-around-flaky-test as acceptable pattern. No performance claim pattern without baseline + boundary. Sources section cites at least two external works.",

      {
        "issue_id": "ddx-13ff86c6",
        "depends_on_id": "ddx-5241bb19",
        "type": "blocks",
        "created_at": "2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"
      }
    ]
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-15f7ee0b",
    "title": "Test runs pollute ~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json with thousands of /tmp/Test.../001 projects",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-23T04:07:09.859726773Z",
    "labels": [
      "feat-020",
      "feat-008",
      "testing",
      "hygiene",
      "perf-amplifier"
    ],
    "description": "## Observed\n\n`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:\n\n- **6,936 named \"001\"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.\n- **344 named \"002\"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.\n- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).\n- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are \"alive\" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.\n- State file size: 1.8 MB.\n\n## Root cause\n\n1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.\n2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.\n3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.\n\n## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)\n\nBoth `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree independently-landable fixes:\n\n### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)\n\n- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).\n- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.\n- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.\n\n### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)\n\n- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.\n- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\\w+\\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.\n- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.\n\n### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI\n\n- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-\u003ctimestamp\u003e`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).\n- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.\n- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Moving state to SQLite.\n- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)\n- Renaming \"001\" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.",
    "acceptance": "**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**\n   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.\n   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.\n\n2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**\n   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:\n     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`\n     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\\d+/` anywhere in the path\n   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).\n   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).\n   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.\n\n3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**\n   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.\n   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-\u003cYYYYMMDDTHHMMSS\u003e` before overwriting.\n   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.\n   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.\n\n4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**\n   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)\n   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.\n\n5. **Backward compatibility.**\n   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.\n   - On upgrade, a note is logged: \"Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file.\"\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.",
    "notes": "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**AC 1 (Fix A):** No changes to any `*_test.go` files to isolate `ServerState` construction, and no lint test added.\n**AC 2 (Fix B):** No changes to `cli/internal/server/state.go` (`ServerState.migrate`) to add test-dir pattern matching or immediate-drop logic. No unit test with seeded fixture state.\n**AC 3 (Fix C):** No `ddx server state prune` command added in `cli/cmd/`. No backup/dry-run logic. No integration test.\n**AC 4 (Perf):** No regression test asserting `GetBeadSnapshots` skips tombstoned/filtered projects.\n**AC 5 (Compat):** No upgrade log message implemented.\n**AC 6 (Cross-ref):** No bead notes linking to `ddx-9ce6842a` or `ddx-2ceb02fa`.\nThe `result.json` reports `\"outcome\": \"task_succeeded\"` but the merged commit `af88d432` (referenced as `result_rev`) had message \"fix(server): drop phantom test-dir projects from state file\" — the actual implementation may have been merged already but is not visible in this diff. The review diff only covers `034fbf8f`, which is the evidence commit, not the implementation commit.\n\nCross-reference: This state-file pollution amplifies `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed). Their fixture sizing assumptions are correct for a clean state file; operator experience on already-polluted machines remains slow until `ddx server state prune` is run.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n`cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` — no test isolation migration or CI lint test is present in the diff.\n`cli/internal/server/state.go`, `ServerState.migrate` — no test-dir pattern filtering, immediate-drop behavior, or phantom-project pruning log is present.\n`cli/internal/server/state_test.go` or equivalent — no sweep fixture test for real, missing `/tmp/Test...`, reachable `/tmp/Test...`, and `/private/tmp/Test...` projects is present.\n`cli/cmd/` or server command wiring — no `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` implementation is present.\nCLI integration tests — no prune fixture test asserting 10 kept entries and a 110-entry backup is present.\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go`, `GetBeadSnapshots` tests, or CI workflow files — no regression test or CI assertion for skipping tombstoned/pattern-filtered projects is present.\nBead metadata/notes — no cross-reference notes linking `ddx-9ce6842a` and `ddx-2ceb02fa` are present in the reviewed diff."
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-18c7de20",
    "title": "agent: suppress no-op compaction telemetry (compaction.start/end pair when nothing was compacted)",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 3,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T20:00:25.588083479Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T20:10:38.114771565Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:telemetry",
      "kind:noise"
    ],
    "description": "Embedded agent emits a compaction.start + compaction.end event pair around EVERY turn (pre-turn check + post-turn check), even when no compaction is performed. Observed in session log .ddx/executions/20260418T191746-684f612a/embedded/agent-1776539866960442383.jsonl during a bragi qwen3.6-35b-a3b run on bead ddx-8610020e:\n\n  {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",\"data\":{\"messages_before\":1}}\n  {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",\"data\":{\"messages_after\":1,\"messages_before\":1,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n  {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",\"data\":{\"messages_before\":6,\"mid_turn\":true}}\n  {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",\"data\":{\"messages_after\":6,\"messages_before\":6,\"mid_turn\":true,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n  ... and so on every turn\n\nBoth fields together (no_compaction:true, success:false) when messages_before == messages_after make this a NO-OP. The event is documenting that the compactor was asked, looked at the input, and decided nothing needed compacting. That is not interesting telemetry.\n\nReal-world impact:\n  - Misleads operators (\"why is compaction firing constantly? — am I context-bound?\") when the actual signal is \"no compaction needed, all is well\"\n  - Inflates session-log size for no diagnostic value\n  - Triggered the false hypothesis on 2026-04-18 that compaction-thrash was the bottleneck on cheap-tier beads (real bottleneck was per-turn latency)\n\nFix options:\n  1. Suppress the start+end pair entirely when the compactor decides no_compaction. Only log when work was actually performed (messages_after \u003c messages_before, OR success:true with measurable token reduction).\n  2. Log a single compaction.skip event instead of the start+end pair, with reason (\"below threshold\", \"nothing summarizable\", etc.). Smaller but still findable for operators who want to confirm the check ran.\n  3. Demote no-op events to a lower verbosity tier (only emit when a debug flag is set).\n\nLikely lives in the ddx-agent compaction package upstream — but the symptom shows up in DDx session logs and operator UX, so DDx should own filing the upstream issue and consuming the fix when it lands. If the no-op log line is purely a DDx-side wrapper, fix here.",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] After the fix, a session that performs zero actual compactions emits zero compaction.start/end pairs (or at most one compaction.skip per turn at low verbosity)\n- [ ] When compaction does perform real work (messages_after \u003c messages_before OR token count drops), events are still emitted with the same data shape they have today\n- [ ] Operator-facing docs (if any) updated to reflect the new event semantics\n- [ ] Regression test in the appropriate package asserts no-op compactions do not emit events at the default verbosity level",
    "notes": "Moved upstream to agent-22c95151 (compaction package lives in ddx-agent library, not in DDx). Closing here."
  },

    "description": "PROBLEM\nExecute-loop execution fields are duplicated across cobra extraction, REST/GraphQL request structs, server ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec persistence, and runWorker consumption. Adding a flag can silently drop it on one transport.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as a server-local struct while cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts a wider flag set, cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 has another request struct, and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 has a third. The type boundary is not shared.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add a small import-safe package under cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ that defines ExecuteLoopSpec with JSON tags and fields ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, and SpecVersion.\n- Add DispatchOptions with only Local and JSON control-plane fields; do not persist it.\n- Add Duration JSON support that marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.\n- Add ApplyDefaults and Validate methods, including SpecVersion support and a code comment documenting the single-version client/server forward-compat stance.\n- Do not wire cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers in this bead; later children consume this package.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not rename CLI flags or change LabelFilter from string.\n- Do not change execute-loop validation policy beyond moving existing checks behind the spec lifecycle.\n- Do not modify server or cobra call sites except imports needed by tests.",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package exists with ExecuteLoopSpec struct (fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion) and JSON tags.\n2. DispatchOptions struct exists with Local and JSON control-plane fields only (not persisted).\n3. Duration JSON support marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.\n4. ApplyDefaults and Validate methods present; SpecVersion single-version forward-compat comment added.\n5. TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTrip_AllFields verifies JSON marshal/unmarshal round-trip preserves all fields.\n6. TestExecuteLoopSpec_ApplyDefaults verifies zero-value fields get correct defaults.\n7. No cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers modified in this bead (wired by later children).\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/executeloop/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

        "depends_on_id": "ddx-29058e2a",

        "created_at": "2026-05-04T02:20:34Z"

  {
    "id": "ddx-1bd14bb3",
    "title": "lefthook conflicts hook false-positive when ddx work runs detached (tty unavailable)",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T19:22:46.225946513Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T19:27:28.726433402Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "area:tooling",
      "area:exec",
      "kind:flake",
      "blocks:execute-loop"
    ],
    "description": "During a `ddx work --local --provider bragi --model qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b` drain on 2026-04-18T19:17, the loop tried to commit a tracker update for ddx-91bc770a (no_changes outcome). lefthook pre-commit blocked it with:\n\n  🥊 conflicts: Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing. (0.00 seconds): exit status 1\n\nBut:\n  1. Verified no file in the repo matches lefthooks own regex `^\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c[ \\t]|^=======$|^\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e[ \\t]|^\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|[ \\t]` — including .ddx/beads.jsonl and the staged execution evidence dir.\n  2. Every OTHER lefthook check (ddx-validate, large-files, secrets, resolution-path-citations, secrets) passed cleanly.\n  3. lefthook output earlier in the run contained: `open /dev/pts/0: permission denied` — strongly suggests lefthook is mis-handling its absence of a controlling tty when ddx work runs detached as a background worker.\n  4. The conflicts grep ran in 0.00s and returned exit 1 — too fast to be a real grep failure on real input. Probably a non-grep failure mode (maybe lefthook fallback path, or empty {staged_files} expansion combined with no stdin).\n\nThe hook command per lefthook.yml is:\n  grep -E \"^\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c[ \\t]|^=======$|^\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e[ \\t]|^\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|[ \\t]\" {staged_files} 2\u003e/dev/null \u0026\u0026 exit 1 || exit 0\n\nThis blocks execute-loop tracker commits non-deterministically and leaves beads in an inconsistent state (claimed/heartbeated but not closed/unclaimed). Must be either fixed in lefthook config (more robust hook), bypassed when ddx work owns the commit (the loop already validates its own tracker writes), or both.",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] Reproduce: invoke a tracker commit via ddx work --local in a detached environment (no /dev/pts/0) and trigger the false positive, OR demonstrate the failure mode in a unit/integration test\n- [ ] Root cause documented (lefthook tty handling, {staged_files} expansion under no-tty, or other)\n- [ ] Fix applied: rewrite the conflicts hook command to be tty-independent (e.g. wrap grep with explicit empty-input handling, or switch to a small script that reads staged file list from git diff --cached --name-only) — and/or have ddx work bypass lefthook for its own tracker commits with a documented rationale\n- [ ] Regression test or runbook step that exercises the fix\n- [ ] No reintroduction of the bug across at least one full ddx work --local drain pass"
  },

    "id": "ddx-1cfce460",
    "title": "execute-bead cheap tier cannot resolve catalog models to live LM Studio providers",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-17T02:49:32.207204061Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-17T02:58:29.941739396Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:build",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:routing",
      "goal:throughput-per-dollar"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-4b59b816",
    "description": "Observed on 2026-04-17T02:39 during an execute-loop --once cycle against bead\nhelix-ede8085a (from /Users/erik/Projects/helix). Evidence preserved at\n/Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023917-dc4a49d3/result.json\nand /Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023921-e66e9375/result.json.\n\nddx agent check --verbose on this host reports live LM Studio providers:\nbragi (10 models), grendel (3), vidar (11), vidar-coder (11), openrouter (345).\nSo model discovery IS working — the gap is in execute-bead's catalog→provider\nresolution.\n\nSymptom 1 — cheap tier / embedded-openai surface\n  Routing decision: tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen3.5-27b\n  Failure: \"model \\\"qwen3.5-27b\\\" did not match any provider, catalog ref, or\n  model-route; specify --provider or --model-ref explicitly\"\n  The user's ~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml maps cheap.embedded-openai → qwen3.5-27b,\n  but the embedded ddx-agent harness has no provider registration that serves\n  qwen3.5-27b. Meanwhile bragi / grendel / vidar all expose lmstudio-backed\n  models and could plausibly serve it — but execute-bead never consults live\n  provider discovery when a catalog model-ref fails to resolve.\n\nSymptom 2 — lmstudio harness fallback\n  Routing decision: harness=lmstudio model=minimax/minimax-m2.7\n  Failure: \"agent: harness lmstudio not available:  not found in PATH\"\n  lmstudio is an HTTP harness (ddx agent doctor reports it as \"(http)\" and OK),\n  yet the execute-bead harness probe path-checks it like a binary CLI and\n  rejects it before any HTTP probe runs.\n\nNet effect: the auto-escalation ladder skips cheap and standard tiers entirely\non this machine and lands on smart-tier codex/gpt-5.4, defeating the\ncost-tiered routing the parent epic (ddx-4b59b816) is designed to enable.\nThe operator should not have to pass --provider vidar or --harness codex to\nroute around this — that re-introduces the hyper-specification this epic is\ntrying to eliminate.\n\nRoot-cause candidates (pick one, not both):\n  a) Extend execute-bead catalog resolution so that when a catalog surface\n     maps to a model-ref the local harness cannot serve, it consults live\n     provider discovery (the same discovery ddx agent check uses) to locate\n     a reachable provider that advertises the model, and routes there.\n  b) Teach ddx agent execute-bead that the lmstudio harness is HTTP-backed\n     and probe it via the live-discovery path instead of PATH lookup, so the\n     cheap-tier fallback from (qwen3.5-27b) → (minimax/minimax-m2.7 via\n     lmstudio) actually reaches the bragi/grendel/vidar endpoints.\n\nBoth symptoms share a root: execute-bead's harness/model resolution has no\nlive-discovery hook. A fix should close that gap.",
    "acceptance": "1. On a host where ddx agent check reports at least one LM Studio provider\n   (bragi, grendel, vidar, vidar-coder) connected, ddx agent execute-loop\n   --once --json against a ready bead routes a cheap-tier attempt to a live\n   provider without requiring --provider or --harness on the CLI.\n2. The result.json for the cheap-tier attempt records status != \"execution_failed\"\n   when the live provider accepts the request (status may be success, no_changes,\n   or a non-dispatch failure downstream, but must NOT be \"did not match any\n   provider, catalog ref, or model-route\" and must NOT be \"harness lmstudio\n   not available: not found in PATH\").\n3. New unit / integration test in the execute-bead routing package asserts:\n   given a catalog entry cheap.embedded-openai: qwen3.5-27b and a discovered\n   provider advertising qwen3.5-27b, the resolver returns that provider.\n4. New unit / integration test asserts: the lmstudio harness is treated as\n   HTTP-probeable, not PATH-probeable, so it survives harness availability\n   checks when the live-discovery path sees an LM Studio provider.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... \u0026\u0026 go build .\n6. ddx agent doctor and ddx agent check exit 0 on a host with live LM Studio\n   providers."
  },

    "id": "ddx-1e516bc9",
    "title": "fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models (upstream gap)",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-05-03T01:06:53.276389609Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-03T01:17:15.675058816Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:fix",
      "upstream-fizeau",
      "observed-failure"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "Upstream fizeau gap: when ServiceExecuteRequest carries an explicit Harness pin AND empty Model AND a non-empty Profile/MinPower, fizeau v0.10.3's Execute (service_execute.go:193-224) treats Harness as a hard pin and skips the routing engine entirely (it only invokes routing when req.Harness == \"\"). Result: request validates with empty Model, fizeau returns Model=\"\", DDx passes no --model arg to the claude CLI, and Anthropic's CLI uses whatever the local user's session default is.\n\nOBSERVED\n- Active worker on ddx-23c67ca4 (PID 4065353): claude --print -p --verbose ... — no --model arg\n- ddx server workers show worker-20260503T004125-0b67 → Model: empty, Harness: claude\n- Operators cannot control which Claude model executes a bead via DDx routing for the claude harness; UI/spec.json reports Model=\"\" (operator sees \"claude\" but not which Claude model)\n\nSCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY\n\nThis bead is HALF A of the original ddx-1e516bc9 problem. HALF B (DDx supplying Profile/MinPower in ServiceExecuteRequest) is owned by ddx-cfedee8e + C9 (ddx-9228a484), which wire the ladder into the executor closure and bump MinPower per each call. Without the ladder wiring, fizeau wouldn't have anything to route on even if this fix lands. With the ladder wiring AND this fizeau fix, the full path works.\n\nINVESTIGATION FIRST\nConfirm the gap before implementing. The hypothesis is that fizeau v0.10.3 service_execute.go:193-224 only runs routing when Harness == \"\". If that's wrong (e.g., fizeau already routes within harness when Profile/MinPower is supplied), this bead is a no-op and gets closed as already-resolved.\n\nUPSTREAM CHANGE (if confirmed)\nModify fizeau's Execute so that when Harness is set and Model is empty and Profile or MinPower is non-empty, the routing engine runs WITHIN that harness's eligible models. Fizeau already has the catalog (models.yaml: opus-4.7 code-high :31-60; sonnet-4.6 code-medium :141-170; claude-haiku-5.5 code-economy :222-251) and harness eligibility (registry.go: claude/codex AutoRoutingEligible=true; gemini/opencode/pi false). The change is: skip the \"Harness != '' means skip routing\" gate when Model is empty AND a routing input (Profile/MinPower) is present.\n\nFor Class 2 harnesses (gemini/opencode/pi, AutoRoutingEligible=false): the engine should still surface a clear \"no auto-resolution available\" error — NOT silently return empty Model. DDx should treat that as a fail-fast on the attempt.\n\nDDx-SIDE WORK (verification only — not implementation)\nOnce the ladder is wired (cfedee8e → C9), DDx will already submit Profile/MinPower for each Execute call. This bead's verification phase asserts that with cfedee8e landed AND fizeau bumped to the version with this fix, ddx work --harness=claude produces a non-empty resolved model in spec.json / result.json / server worker UI.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- DDx-side ladder wiring or MinPower-supply changes (owned by ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-9228a484 C9)\n- Operator-explicit --model passes (already work; pinned by operator intent)\n- HTTP providers openrouter/lmstudio (no subprocess --model arg; not subprocess invocations)\n- Stale resolveModel() in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:380 (legacy path; codex confirmed production uses RunWithConfigViaService → svc.Execute, not the legacy Run() path)\n\nWHY P1 (was P0)\nDemoted because:\n- The DDx-side enabling work (cfedee8e/C9) is already P1/P2 in the queue\n- This bead's effect is only observable after cfedee8e/C9 land\n- It's an upstream fizeau bump, not a DDx code change\n- Original P0 framing assumed a DDx-side fix in the dispatch path; that part is the refactor's job\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-cfedee8e (or its absorber ddx-9228a484 C9): the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on",
    "acceptance": "1. Investigation phase. Read fizeau v0.10.3 source (cli/go.mod pin) and confirm whether explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower actually skips routing OR runs routing within the harness. Evidence committed at .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/fizeau-execute-routing-investigation.md with file:line citations into fizeau's source. Outcome A: gap confirmed → continue; Outcome B: gap doesn't exist → close bead as already-resolved with the report as evidence.\n\n2. (Outcome A only) Upstream fizeau change: file an issue/PR against github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau so that explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower triggers the routing engine within that harness's eligible models. For Class 2 harnesses (AutoRoutingEligible=false: gemini/opencode/pi), the engine returns an explicit \"no auto-resolution available for harness=X\" error, NOT silent empty Model.\n\n3. (Outcome A only) Bump fizeau in cli/go.mod once the upstream change ships.\n\n4. Integration test (in fizeau, or DDx-side stub if upstream test coverage is sufficient): TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness — assert ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:\"claude\", Model:\"\", Profile:\"code-economy\"} → response RoutingActual.Model is non-empty AND is a member of the claude family per catalog.\n\n5. Companion negative test: TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly — assert empty Model + empty Profile/MinPower returns a clear \"under-specified routing\" error instead of silently empty Model.\n\n6. End-to-end verification (after cfedee8e/C9 also landed): ddx work --harness=claude on a small queue. Assert via:\n   - .ddx/workers/\u003cid\u003e/spec.json: Model field non-empty after first attempt\n   - .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/result.json: routing_decision evidence shows resolved model from the claude family\n   - ddx server workers show \u003cid\u003e: Model field non-empty\n   - NOT via process-list inspection\n\n7. Use current catalog model names in tests/assertions: opus-4.7, sonnet-4.6, claude-haiku-5.5 (NOT stale Haiku/Sonnet/Opus literals). Fizeau may normalize to family aliases (haiku/sonnet/opus) for the actual --model arg via service_models.go:164-185.\n\n8. NO local-config \"fallback when fizeau unavailable\" path. Empty fizeau response = fail the attempt; DDx retry policy escalates next attempt.\n\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.\n\n10. Documentation: code comment on fizeau Execute call site (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go) noting that empty Model + Profile/MinPower is the well-formed request shape and what fizeau is expected to return.",

    "description": "## Status update (filed 2026-04-29 12:50 UTC)\n\nThe bulk of this unification **already shipped** as `ddx-87fb72c2` (epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`) before this epic was filed:\n\n- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides` are now opt-in (consulted only with `--escalate` / `--override-model`).\n- The default execute path on the CLI (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`) calls `ResolveRoute` once per attempt instead of iterating a tier ladder.\n- `agent.routing.default_harness` was removed (hard error if still present).\n- `agent.ResolveTierCandidates` (from `ddx-5538aa5b`) replaced the broken literal-tier-name-as-Model pattern; tier resolution now consults the catalog.\n- See `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` for the migration the user-facing CLI surface follows.\n\n**Remaining gap (this epic now scopes only this):** the server worker path in `cli/internal/server/workers.go:650` was not updated by `ddx-87fb72c2`. It still derives `escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 spec.Model == \"\"` instead of mirroring the CLI's `escalate \u0026\u0026 harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 model == \"\"`. That mismatch is why `ddx work` (default mode, server-submit) still hits \"all tiers exhausted — no viable provider\" with `model_overrides` pointing at non-served models, while `ddx work --local --no-adaptive-min-tier` succeeds. Verified live in this session: `worker-20260429T033325-bedf` failed; `--no-adaptive-min-tier` got `ddx-98e6e9ef` to merge at `115b47c0` on local-tier qwen for zero cost.\n\nSole child: `ddx-c7081f89`. Once that lands, this epic closes.\n\n## Strategic direction (preserved for future reference)\n\nDDx embeds the agent SDK as a Go library to produce traceable execution units: `(bead, input_state) → (output_state, processing_metadata)`. DDx owns the execution unit and its metadata (bead events, cost ledger, escalation summary, evidence directories), and it owns operator policy (`--harness`/`--model`/`--profile`/`--max-cost`/`--no-review`/`--escalate`). It does **not** need to own provider/harness/cost/local-first routing — agent SDK already does that.\n\n| Concern | DDx (now, post-87fb72c2) | Agent SDK |\n|---|---|---|\n| Profile selection | `RouteRequest.Profile` straight to agent | `default/cheap/standard/smart/local/offline/air-gapped/fast/code-smart/code-high` |\n| Per-tier model pinning | opt-in via `--override-model` | `RouteRequest.Model` |\n| Escalation on no-provider | opt-in via `--escalate` (CLI path); **not yet on server worker path** | `escalateProfileLadder` walks `ProfileEscalationLadder=[cheap,standard,smart]` on `ErrNoLiveProvider` |\n| Cost-aware ranking | n/a (delegated) | `internal/routing/score.go` ranks by `CostClass` (local=0 wins) |\n| Adaptive success-rate gating | `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` (sticky 50-window cheap-tier lockout, only consumed via `--escalate`) | `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess map[harness]float64` |\n| Decision trace | bead events `kind:tier-attempt` per RouteCandidate | `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` |\n\n## Optional follow-ups (not in this epic — file separately if/when needed)\n\nThese are no longer blockers; track separately if pursued:\n\n- **Default profile = `local`** so the operator-default honors the local-LLM-first goal without an explicit `--profile local`. Currently default is `default` which maps via project config.\n- **Inject `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess` per-harness** to replace `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` with agent-native success-rate routing. Frees DDx from maintaining its own success-window state machine.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go` helpers** once no caller (CLI or server) consumes them. Currently consumed only via `--escalate`; if `--escalate` is dropped or moved into agent, these can go.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go::AdaptiveMinTier`** once the HistoricalSuccess injection above is in place.\n\n## Out of scope for this epic\n\n- Changes to agent SDK source — if a gap is found, file an upstream bead under `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.\n- UI/e2e work — the parent e2e drift epic `ddx-ccdf9cf9` covers that and is independent.\n- Server source changes outside `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — no API endpoint changes, no GraphQL schema changes.",

  {
    "id": "ddx-42dd982c",
    "title": "execute-loop: divergence monitoring and alerting",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-04-14T12:21:10.525089812Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-14T15:56:12.030389438Z",
    "labels": [
      "execute-loop",
      "monitoring",
      "observability"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-b7ee0d6f",
    "description": "Fifth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The 14-day silent divergence on axon (eitri local 134 commits ahead of merge-base, sindri/origin 336 commits ahead) was caught only because a human happened to try pushing. A trivial monitor running a few times a day would have flagged the problem at 5 commits, not 470.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nAdd a lightweight divergence check that runs either:\n- At the top of each execute-loop iteration (cheap, ~50ms), OR\n- Periodically via a scheduled task (ddx doctor or similar), OR\n- Both\n\nThe check computes:\n- `git fetch origin \u003cbranch\u003e` (tolerant of network failure)\n- local_ahead = `git rev-list --count origin/\u003cbranch\u003e..HEAD`\n- origin_ahead = `git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/\u003cbranch\u003e`\n- merge_base_age = `git log -1 --format=%ar $(git merge-base HEAD origin/\u003cbranch\u003e)`\n\nEmit a structured event 'divergence_status' with these fields. If:\n- local_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- origin_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- both \u003e 0: ERROR — diverged, neither side is a strict ancestor\n- merge_base_age \u003e threshold (default 7 days): warn (drift)\n\nIntegration surface:\n- `ddx doctor` prints the divergence report\n- `ddx agent execute-loop` refuses to start if in the ERROR state (both sides ahead) unless --force is passed\n- An optional webhook / notification channel (config: alert_webhook_url) for ops teams\n\n## Why this is P1 not P0\n\nThe fetch/push/rebase triad (P0) prevents NEW divergence. This bead catches EXISTING divergence and alerts before it grows. Without the P0 fixes, this alert would fire constantly; with them, it should fire rarely. Still valuable as defense in depth.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Fresh clone: report is clean (0 ahead / 0 behind)\n- Local 3 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=3, no warning\n- Local 6 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=6, warning emitted\n- Both sides ahead: ERROR status, execute-loop refuses to start\n- Merge base 10 days old with both sides at 0: drift warning, execute-loop continues\n- Network unreachable: report shows 'unknown', no ERROR, execute-loop continues",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] New command or subcommand: `ddx doctor divergence` or `ddx divergence check` prints the divergence report\n- [ ] Thresholds are configurable via .ddx/config.yaml (default: 5 commits each side, 7 days merge-base age)\n- [ ] execute-loop refuses to start in ERROR state unless --force passed\n- [ ] Structured event 'divergence_status' emitted at loop start and on every doctor run\n- [ ] Optional webhook integration via config.yaml alert_webhook_url\n- [ ] Unit tests: each state (clean, ahead, behind, diverged, network-down, drift)\n- [ ] Integration test: run the loop on a diverged repo and observe the refuse-to-start behavior\n- [ ] Docs: README section on divergence monitoring, thresholds, and how to recover"
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-4375292b",
    "title": "agent: invoke runner-backed post-attempt triage hook",
    "status": "in_progress",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-05-04T15:37:15.580462478Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-05T04:07:14.431149798Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:feature",
      "reliability",
      "bead-quality",
      "adr:023"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e1a576a7",
    "description": "PROBLEM\n  ddx try needs post-attempt triage after the loop has adjudicated an attempt outcome and before cooldown decisions, so transient failures can be classified without mutating the underlying status. Today the loop records status/detail/rationale but has no triage hook invocation path that can populate OutcomeReason.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:940-1090 contains the outcome adjudication and cooldown branch chain. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:976 and 1084 call shouldSuppressNoProgress before applying no-progress cooldown. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1099 appends the report to the loop result after cooldown handling. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:30 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook and cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines TriageResult, but no cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go exists and the loop never calls the hook.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Add cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go with a constructor, e.g. NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, sessionLogReader SessionLogExcerptReader) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error). If package boundaries require a different name, preserve the explicit injected inputs.\n  - The hook builds a prompt whose first line is MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes the bead body, ExecuteBeadReport as JSON, the most relevant recent bead events, and a bounded session-log excerpt when report.SessionID is non-empty and a log can be found under the project agent log dir.\n  - Invoke dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: prompt, WorkDir: projectRoot, PromptSource: \"bead-lifecycle-triage\"}) or an equivalent package-local wrapper that still uses AgentRunner/ResolvedConfig and not os/exec.\n  - In execute_bead_loop.go, call runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook after report.Status/Detail/Rationale/BaseRev/ResultRev are final for the attempt and before any shouldSuppressNoProgress or SetExecutionCooldown branch. A valid classification sets report.OutcomeReason = TriageResult.Classification and records triage details in the execute-bead event body or a dedicated bead-quality.triage event.\n  - On hook failure, fail open: leave OutcomeReason empty and keep legacy cooldown behavior.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - Defining OutcomeReason and TriageResult; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.\n  - CLI wiring for ddx try; covered by sibling/parent wiring work.\n  - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.\n  - Automatically creating SuggestedFollowupBeads; record/surface only unless a later bead opts in.\n\nPARENT\n  ddx-e1a576a7.\n\nDEPS\n  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and the OutcomeReason/TriageResult contract exists.",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go implements NewPostAttemptTriageHook or an equivalently named constructor returning func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error), with explicit injected AgentRunner/service/config inputs and no dependency on cli/cmd.\n2. TestTriageHook_UsesRunnerLibrary asserts the hook reaches an injected AgentRunner.Run path and a static grep/AST assertion proves triage_hook.go does not import os/exec or shell out to ddx agent run.\n3. TestTriageHook_PromptIncludesOutcomeAndLogExcerpt verifies the prompt starts with MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes bead content, ExecuteBeadReport JSON, and a bounded session-log excerpt when available.\n4. TestLoop_TriageHook_FiresPostOutcome proves PostAttemptTriageHook runs after report status/detail are populated and before no-progress cooldown is applied.\n5. TestTriageHook_RecordsButDoesNotMutateOutcome proves valid triage classification populates OutcomeReason and/or a triage event without changing ExecuteBeadReport.Status.\n6. TestTriageHook_HookError_FallsThroughToLegacyCooldown proves hook error leaves OutcomeReason empty and preserves existing cooldown behavior.\n7. The triage prompt requests structured JSON fields classification, recommended_action, rationale, suggested_amendments, and suggested_followup_beads.\n8. WIRED-IN: git grep PostAttemptTriageHook cli/internal/agent shows the execute-loop invocation site, not just the runtime field declaration.\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green.\n10. lefthook run pre-commit passes."
  },

    "id": "ddx-43c8bf3c",
    "title": "ddx doctor should verify running binary is in canonical install location",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-04-14T03:08:24.081395593Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-14T04:25:25.403998005Z",
    "description": "install.sh installs the ddx binary to $HOME/.local/bin/ddx as the canonical location. But users can end up with multiple copies (e.g. /home/erik/bin/ddx from a stale install, /usr/local/bin/ddx, or a dev build on PATH ahead of the canonical one). This session had a real incident: /home/erik/bin/ddx was 053171bf (older) while /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx was 40f8db0d (current) — the shell picked the wrong one for hours, causing confusion about whether blocker fixes were actually active. ddx doctor should detect mismatches between the running binary's path and the canonical install location AND warn when multiple ddx copies exist on PATH with different SHAs.",
    "acceptance": "ddx doctor (1) reports the absolute path of the currently-executing binary via os.Executable(); (2) checks whether that path matches $HOME/.local/bin/ddx (the install.sh canonical location); (3) walks PATH and reports every other 'ddx' found, flagging any whose SHA-256 differs from the running binary as a warning; (4) suggests 'rm \u003cstale\u003e \u0026\u0026 cp \u003ccurrent\u003e \u003cstale\u003e' or 're-run install.sh' to fix. Test covers the multi-binary-on-PATH scenario by shadowing a fake ddx in a temp dir earlier on PATH."
  },

This is error-prone and loses the atomicity guarantee that a reopen is a single logical event.\n\n## Scope\n- ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e [--reason \u003ctext\u003e] [--append-notes \u003ctext\u003e]\n- Atomically: set status=open, append a reopen event to the event log, optionally append notes, clear claim fields\n- Emit an event of kind=reopen that shows in ddx bead show\n- Works from CLI and from ddx agent execute-loop review-rejection path\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: first attempt merged, opus review rejected, I had to run three ddx bead update commands plus a description rewrite to get the bead back into a ready-for-second-attempt state. An atomic reopen would have been one command.",

    "description": "Implement the remaining routing mode changes from SD-015: mode 5 (--provider\nalone), provider-as-modifier semantics, and harness name collision fix.\n\nDepends on ddx-71fe194f (discovery integration) being landed first — mode 5\nneeds discovery to enumerate models on a provider.\n\nImplementation:\n- Mode 5: --provider P alone (no --model, no --profile) discovers P's models\n  and selects the best cataloged one. Error on unknown/empty provider.\n- Provider as modifier with --model: soft preference via scoring bonus.\n  If preferred provider lacks the model, route to one that has it.\n- Provider as modifier with --profile: same soft preference.\n- Harness name collision: --provider claude with --model set → soft\n  preference (not harness override). Without --model → harness override.\n  Warn on collision.\n- Remove ValidateOrphanModel entirely (replaced by discovery error path).",
    "acceptance": "1. ddx work --provider vidar (no --model) selects best cataloged model from vidar.\n2. ddx work --provider nonexistent errors: \"unknown provider P\".\n3. ddx work --provider vidar when vidar unreachable errors: \"provider P has no available models\".\n4. ddx work --model qwen3.6 --provider vidar prefers vidar; ignores vidar and routes to bragi if vidar lacks it.\n5. ddx work --model qwen3.6 --provider claude treats claude as soft preference (not harness override) because --model is set.\n6. ddx work --provider claude (no --model) treats claude as harness override.\n7. Collision warning printed when --provider shadows a harness name.\n\nUnit tests:\nT1. Provider alone: selects best cataloged model from provider's discovered list.\nT2. Unknown provider: clear error.\nT3. Provider affinity soft: preferred provider ignored when it lacks the model.\nT4. Harness collision with --model: soft preference, not override.\nT5.   {
    "id": "ddx-969c5500",
    "title": "FEAT: agent-logs jsonl schema is metadata-only; add verbose mode capturing tool I/O and content",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "ddx",
    "created_at": "2026-04-29T00:11:54.855038209Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-29T03:26:31.084109036Z",
    "labels": [
      "kind:implementation",
      "area:agent",
      "area:measurement"
    ],
    "description": "## Problem\n\n`.ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-*.jsonl` (lean schema) records only:\n  - `session.start` (model, harness, bead_id)\n  - `llm.response` (turn, input_tokens, output_tokens, elapsed_ms)\n  - `tool.call`     (turn, tool name)\n\nNotably absent: model output content, tool call arguments, tool call results, finish_reason, full usage breakdown (cache reads, cache writes), error/stop reasons.\n\nMeanwhile, `agent/demos/sessions/*.jsonl` from the sibling `Projects/agent` repo demonstrates a richer schema for the same event types:\n\n```jsonl\n{\"type\":\"llm.response\",\"data\":{\"content\":\"...\",\"finish_reason\":\"tool_calls\",\"usage\":{\"input\":899,\"output\":21,\"total\":920},\"latency_ms\":3040,\"tool_calls\":1}}\n{\"type\":\"tool.call\",\"data\":{\"tool\":\"edit\",\"input\":{\"path\":\"...\",\"old_string\":\"...\",\"new_string\":\"...\"},\"output\":\"Replaced 1 occurrence\",\"duration_ms\":0,\"error\":\"\"}}\n```\n\n## Why it matters\n\n- Cannot replay a real session to reproduce a bug — the inputs are gone.\n- Cannot root-cause provider failures (e.g. RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI seen in sessions.jsonl) because the prompt that triggered it is not in the per-event log; only the whole-session metadata in sessions.jsonl has it.\n- The lucebox-hub/dflash `bench_server.py` replay-mode benchmark needs full prompt text from real traces, not just metadata, to be representative.\n- Inability to tell what tool args caused a slow call.\n\n## Proposed fix\n\nAdd a verbose mode to the agent-logs writer (env var `DDX_AGENT_LOG_VERBOSE=1` or config flag) that adopts the agent/demos schema:\n  - llm.response: include `content`, `finish_reason`, full `usage` object\n  - tool.call: include `input` (tool args), `output` (truncated to N KB), `duration_ms`, `error`\n  - new event: `llm.request` with messages_count and possibly truncated message preview\n\nVerbose mode off by default to avoid bloating logs. On for any session with `--debug` or for failed sessions (auto-bump-on-error).\n\n## Side note\n\n`sessions.jsonl` (the 37 MB whole-session log) does have full `prompt` text but no per-turn breakdown. So we have two log formats and neither captures the full picture. This bead is about the per-event log only; whole-session log is out of scope.",
    "acceptance": "1. `DDX_AGENT_LOG_VERBOSE=1 ddx agent ...` produces a trace where llm.response events include `content`, `finish_reason`, `usage`, and tool.call events include `input`, `output`, `duration_ms`, `error`.\n2. Schema documented in cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go (Go doc comment listing all fields per event type for both lean and verbose modes).\n3. Default behavior unchanged (lean mode by default; existing tooling that consumes the lean schema continues to work).\n4. Test fixture added for verbose mode that asserts every required field is populated."
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-9a5df084",
    "title": "Fix pre-push no-debug hook regex compatibility and failure semantics",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-11T01:23:01.461954232Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-14T04:24:48.163261903Z",
    "labels": [
      "helix",
      "phase:build",
      "area:tooling",
      "area:quality"
    ],
    "description": "\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved during `git push` in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx after landing tracker commits for execute-loop alpha findings. The DDx pre-push hook `no-debug` invoked `rg` with a look-ahead pattern and emitted a regex parse error, but the hook summary still reported success and the push continued.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nThe DDx pre-push `no-debug` hook uses an `rg` pattern that requires PCRE2 look-around support, but it invokes plain `rg` without `--pcre2`. In the current environment that produces a parse error while the hook still exits successfully.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`, run `git push`.\n2. Observe pre-push hook output from `no-debug` containing:\n   `rg: regex parse error: ... look-around ... is not supported`\n3. Observe the hook summary still marks `no-debug` as passed and the push continues.\n\n## Why this matters\nThe hook currently gives a false sense of enforcement. A broken regex should either be written in ripgrep-compatible syntax or use `--pcre2`, and failures in the debug scan should not be silently treated as success.",
    "acceptance": "The DDx pre-push `no-debug` hook runs without regex parse errors in a default ripgrep environment; the debug-scan command fails closed when the scan itself errors; and the hook still blocks actual debug statements it is meant to catch."
  },

    "description": "PROBLEM\nNo GraphQL resolver exists for Artifact.typeDefinitions. The ArtifactTypePanel (ddx-4fd71cf3) needs this resolver to show matched type definitions for an artifact. Without it, the frontend cannot retrieve template/prompt/example content for any artifact.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql (or equivalent): Artifact type does not have a typeDefinitions field.\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go: no typeDefinitions resolver function exists for the Artifact type.\n- The ddx library registers ArtifactTypeDefinitions under the library plugin structure, but no resolver maps from a library artifact path to its type definition prefix.\n- File content for template/prompt/example must be loaded on-demand (not at graph build time) with a size cap to avoid loading large files into memory.\n- ddx-1d52a30c (artifact type definition data model, a dep) must land first.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add typeDefinitions: [ArtifactTypeDefinition!]! field to the Artifact type in schema.graphql.\n- Add resolver in graphql_resolver.go that:\n  1. TestTypeDefinitions_PrefixCollision_MultiMatch returns multiple ArtifactTypeDefinitions.\n6.   {
    "id": "ddx-9ce6842a",
    "title": "Bead list and cross-project all-beads are too slow: establish a GraphQL perf harness and fix N·M scan pattern",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-04-22T20:41:52.271330549Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-25T07:36:55.005145669Z",
    "labels": [
      "feat-008",
      "feat-004",
      "perf",
      "graphql"
    ],
    "description": "## Observed\n\nPer-project `/beads` page is noticeably slow; the cross-project \"all beads\" view is substantially slower. Related: `ddx-ad0db8fd` already identified the single-bead-detail slowness caused by the same storage pattern.\n\n## Root cause (same pattern, different call site)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:22,40` —\n\n- `Beads` (cross-project) and `BeadsByProject` (single-project) both call `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(status, label, projectID, search)`.\n- `GetBeadSnapshots` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105`) iterates every registered project, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), filters in Go. Cost is `O(projects × beads_per_project)` on every call, independent of the filter/page size.\n- All filtering (status, label, project, search) is Go-side, post-read. The page-size window is applied in `beadConnectionFromSnapshots` after the full corpus is already materialized.\n- Cross-project \"all beads\" = same loop, just without the projectID filter. It is the worst case by design.\n\nThis is the same shape that's breaking the Sessions feed (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) and single-bead detail (`ddx-ad0db8fd`). A point fix here is insufficient — we need a measurement surface first so we stop relying on anecdote.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Perf harness (foundational; enables everything else)\n\nIntroduce a reusable GraphQL perf harness under `cli/internal/server/perf/` or `cli/internal/server/graphql/bench_test.go`:\n\n- Seeded fixture builder: generates N projects × M beads with configurable distribution of status/labels/dependencies. Reuses existing store primitives (no fake data layer).\n- Go benchmark + scriptable HTTP runner (wrapper around an already-running server) reporting p50/p95/p99 per query shape.\n- Targets covered on day one: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject(projectID, first, after, status, label, search)`, `beads(projectID=nil, first, after, status, label)`, `docGraph`, `sessions` (so the sessions shard work `ddx-2ceb02fa` inherits this harness). New query targets are added by writing a single table row.\n- Thresholds are **recorded, not asserted**, in the first pass. CI surfaces a regression if a threshold is breached beyond a configured delta (e.g., +25% p95). Initial pass is \"land the harness and record baseline\"; a follow-up wires it into CI.\n\n### Part 2 — Fix the scan pattern\n\nMinimum viable, in priority order:\n\n1. **Scope by project when the query is scoped.** `BeadsByProject` should call a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, filters…)` that only opens that one project's store. Trivial change; eliminates the cross-project factor for the common case.\n2. **Early filter pushdown.** `GetBeadSnapshots` should apply status/label/projectID filters at the file-parse boundary where cheap, not after materializing every bead. (Still reads every file — deeper fix below.)\n3. **Lightweight in-memory index with invalidation.** After (1) and (2), if \"all beads\" or search is still slow, introduce an index keyed `{projectID, beadID}` with basic metadata (status, labels, title, updatedAt). Refresh on bead write + debounced filesystem watch. Full-body reads stay lazy — the index never holds bodies. This is the same storage discipline we adopted for sessions in `ddx-2ceb02fa`.\n\nThe harness from Part 1 is the gate for choosing between (2) and (3); don't commit to the index without numbers.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Full-text search infrastructure (tracked separately if needed).\n- Changing the on-disk bead format.\n- Cross-node federation performance. Current concern is a single server reading a single workspace.",
    "acceptance": "**User story:** As a developer using the web UI on a real-world-sized workspace (tens of projects × hundreds of beads each), the beads list opens in under a second and the cross-project all-beads view stays usable. Before any perf fix lands, we can reproduce the problem and measure improvement — not guess.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Perf harness exists and is reusable.**\n   - Fixture builder generates `N × M` beads across projects with configurable shape (status mix, label density, dep count). Reuses the real `bead.Store` write path (no fake writer).\n   - Benchmark covers at minimum: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject`, `beads` (cross-project), `docGraph`, `sessions`. Adding a new target is a single-table-row change.\n   - Each target produces p50/p95/p99 for in-process calls and end-to-end HTTP. Results write to a human-readable report (markdown or JSON) plus a machine-readable artifact suitable for CI diffing later.\n\n2. **Baseline recorded.** First run of the harness on the current `main` binary is stored in the bead notes and in `docs/helix/04-observe/perf/YYYY-MM-DD-baseline.md` (or equivalent existing location). Numbers include hardware + go version footer.\n\n3. **BeadsByProject scope fix.** `BeadsByProject` calls a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, …)` that opens exactly one project's store. Unit test asserts only that project's store is opened (no iteration over other projects).\n\n4. **Filter pushdown.** Status/label/projectID filters apply at the per-file / per-entry parse boundary, not post-materialization. Benchmark captures before/after for the common filtered case (e.g., `status=open`).\n\n5. **Perf targets on fixture (10 projects × 500 beads = 5000 beads total):**\n   - `bead(id:)` p95 ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP (already covered by `ddx-ad0db8fd`; this bead may inherit the work).\n   - `beadsByProject(first=50)` p95 ≤ 75ms in-process / ≤ 250ms HTTP.\n   - `beads(first=50, no projectID)` p95 ≤ 200ms in-process / ≤ 500ms HTTP.\n   These are targets for the **first** pass (scope + pushdown). If they are not met with (1)+(2), escalate to the in-memory index (step 3 of Part 2 in the description) and update these targets with the new baseline.\n\n6. **Stale-read safety.** Regardless of fix chosen, a test writes a bead via `ddx bead update`, then queries it via GraphQL, then asserts the new value is visible on the very next request. Works under concurrency: N concurrent readers + writers interleaved without torn reads or panics.\n\n7. **CI posture (soft).** The harness is runnable via `make bench-graphql` (or the project's equivalent). CI wiring to fail on \u003e25% regression is a follow-up bead — recorded in this bead's notes with the proposed threshold but not implemented here.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** Opening `/beads` on the fixture project returns an interactive (clickable) list within 1s. Opening the cross-project `/beads` page returns within 2s. These are ceilings, not p95 targets — guards against outright regression, not fine-grained perf.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead's notes list `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions shard/index) as the other two bead/session perf threads that should inherit this harness.",
    "notes": "## 2026-04-22 — User directives on fixture size and storage strategy\n\nTwo standing instructions from the user that apply to this bead and to all other perf beads (`ddx-ad0db8fd`, `ddx-2ceb02fa`, and any spawned from efficacy/providers work):\n\n1. **Minimum fixture size: 1,000s of records.** The original acceptance criterion (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000) already meets this for beads, but restate the floor explicitly: **no perf work lands against a fixture smaller than 1,000 records per query shape**. Sessions fixtures, efficacy fixtures, provider/quota history fixtures — all ≥1k rows. Beads fixture stays at 5k; sessions fixture for `ddx-2ceb02fa` should be ≥10k index entries (already specified); efficacy and any new surfaces ≥1k attempts/rows each.\n\n2. **DB backend is on the table.** If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, lightweight index) cannot hit the target latencies on the fixture sizes above, the user is open to introducing a real data store — candidates named: **axon**, **sqlite**, **postgres**. Do not silently pick one: **raise the decision with the user before choosing a path.** Capture the baseline, capture what in-place optimization achieved, then bring the choice (and the rationale) to the user.\n\nThese directives supersede any acceptance criterion that implies \"optimize in place no matter what.\" If the numbers say storage needs to change, that's the outcome — but the user decides the substrate.\n\n**AC §8 — Playwright cross-project test routes wrong path**: `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigates to `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/beads` for the \"cross-project\" test, which is the same per-project URL as the 1s test. A cross-project smoke test should hit the all-beads route (e.g., `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/beads` or whatever the actual cross-project URL is) and mock the `beads` query, not `beadsByProject`. The mock for `beads` exists (lines 76-89) but is never exercised because both tests navigate to the project-scoped path.\n**AC §9 — Bead notes cross-reference**: The acceptance criteria require this bead's notes to list `ddx-ad0db8fd` and `ddx-2ceb02fa`. Environment: `go=go1.26.2 goos=linux goarch=arm64 cpu-cores=12`.\n\nKey p95 results: `bead` 6.72ms in-process / 6.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 8.84ms in-process / 9.29ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.16ms in-process / 75.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.45ms in-process / 6.87ms HTTP.\n\nCross-reference for inherited harness work: `ddx-ad0db8fd` covers single-bead detail lookup; `ddx-2ceb02fa` covers sessions shard/index work. Both should use/extend this GraphQL perf harness.\n\nReview follow-up: AC §8 cross-project Playwright smoke now navigates to `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercises the `beads` GraphQL query instead of the project-scoped `beadsByProject` path.     "description": "Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.\nSuggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.",
    "acceptance": "SD-015 defines a stable model-level attribution key for routing metrics so RoutingOutcome, QuotaSnapshot, and any derived burn/performance summaries can be associated with the resolved candidate model or canonical target; FEAT-014 stays consistent with that key; and the docs no longer imply that latency/cost/reliability from different models on the same surface are mixed into one routing signal"
  },

    "priority": 0,

      "review-finding"
    ],
    "description": "Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/internal/git/autocommit.go:65\nCategory: integration\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: AutoCommit now changes the git working directory to filepath.Dir(filePath) but still runs git add with the original filePath. That works for absolute paths like .ddx/beads.jsonl in bead close, but it breaks existing relative nested-path callers. For example, from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, running `cd cli \u0026\u0026 git add -n cli/cmd/bead.go` fails with `fatal: pathspec 'cli/cmd/bead.go' did not match any files`, because the path is interpreted relative to the new cwd as cli/cli/cmd/bead.go. cli/cmd/doc.go passes relative document paths into AutoCommit, so doc stamping auto-commit will now fail for nested docs paths.\\nSuggested fix: Normalize filePath before staging when AutoCommit changes directories. Either resolve filePath to an absolute path and convert it to a repo-root-relative path for `git add`, or keep the cwd at the repo root and only change it for repo detection/rev-parse.",
    "acceptance": "With git.auto_commit=always, auto-committing a nested relative path succeeds without a git pathspec error, and a regression test covers a relative-path caller such as ddx doc stamp or AutoCommit itself"
  },
